I think what I liked most about these shoes is they feel like they are built to last. These will be a regular in the rotation

Great Stabilizing Gym Shoe!. The Nike Air Zoom Bella 7 shoes are a great combination of style and functionality. The gray color is versatile and pairs well with so much in my wardrobe, making them a go-to for both casual and workout attire. They provide excellent stability, especially during exercises like lunges and squats—my balance feels spot on. They’re great gym shoes overall, offering strong support during weight training.  However, I wouldn’t recommend them for running or activities that require a lot of foot flexibility. They don’t offer the same level of cushioning or flexibility you’d need for those types of exercises. One thing to note - they do run a bit small, so I’d suggest sizing up for a more comfortable fit.  Overall, they’re a solid choice for gym use and everyday wear, just size up and keep in mind their limitations for high-impact or flexibility-focused activities

Decent Shoe. This is a decent shoe overall. It does run big in my opinion, specifically in the heel. The shape and look is really sleek and nice. There was not a lot of structure to the front of the shoe compared to the heel, which made it feel a bit uneven. The fabric is a mesh, but does stain easily, water even leaves a mark which was frustrating. The footbed had a very interesting molding and I would recommend inserts for your own feet. They do not have a mold pattern to a normal foot and with the arch having more support, which did cause some unevenness with wear. The colors were nice and light and the shoe looks really good. I got compliments wearing them, from an aesthetic perspective they were great. They were a fairly light shoe as well, not heavy or bulky which made the wear easier given the other issues. Over all the shoe isn’t a bad shoe, it isn’t a shoe for me to wear for exercise or sports, maybe leisure, but with some tweaks it could be an incredible shoe

Best for narrow feet. The Nike Bella 7 is a stylish and functional athletic shoe that excels in both performance and everyday wear. Its snug yet comfortable fit provides excellent support without feeling restrictive, while its contoured design and cushioned insole ensure long-lasting comfort. However, those with wider feet may find them slightly narrow.  Nike’s signature style shines through in the Bella 7, offering a range of appealing color options that maintain a fun and sporty aesthetic. The upper material, composed of breathable mesh, keeps your feet cool during workouts, while its lightweight and durable nature allows for easy movement. However, it may not be the best choice for colder weather due to its lack of insulation.  The standout feature of the Bella 7 is its perfect blend of comfort and style. It’s an ideal shoe for both training and everyday use, boasting excellent traction and cushioning.  Personally, I believe the sizing could be more accommodating for wider feet, and the laces feel slightly thin, requiring frequent retying unless double-knotted.  In conclusion, the Nike Bella 7 is a solid choice for those seeking a comfortable, stylish, and functional athletic shoe

[This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I’m impressed with the overall quality of these shoes—the materials feel durable, the fit is secure, and the upper is both breathable and supportive. They definitely feel like a premium, well-constructed running shoe.  That said, the cushioning level feels higher than advertised. Even though the shoe is described as having a “medium” cushion, it runs closer to a high-cushion feel underfoot. For runners who love that extra softness, this could be perfect. But for me, it felt like a little too much—almost like I was sinking into the shoe rather than getting a balanced, responsive ride.  If you’re someone who wants maximum shock absorption, this shoe will deliver. But if you’re looking for a true medium cushion with more ground feel, you may find it a bit on the plush side

Great shoe, a bit on the heavy side.. [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I bought this shoe for the second half of summer mileage for fall marathon training and I was pleased with the underfoot feel, however it is a bit on the heavy side. This shoe is great for recovery and longer mileage, but starts to get heavy further into the run. The upper is extremely comfortable and the padded tongue reduces any lace bite for someone who likes their shoes tied fairly tight. Most of the weight that I noticed falls at the bottom of the foot towards the outsole and it can affect your stride and footstrike if you are fatigued. I have been able to pick up the pace for some progression runs, but not without some effort. The nice thing about running hard efforts in this shoe is that when switching to lighter, tempo shoes, the weight reduction is noticeable and I have been able to notice the effort is drastically reduced when pushing faster paces. Overall, I would recommend this as a recovery/easy run shoe and possibly a daily trainer if you are looking to run for fitness. It's a great shoe to have in rotation and it will definitely help you develop some speed if you push the pace in training then switch to a light shoe for hard efforts and race day

So much more responsive and comfortable than the Pegasus or others in this general price (+/- £35) range, but terrible production planning by Nike. Sold out for months despite heavy demand and nothing close as an immediate substitute.  If they can improve the longevity then you’ll satisfy a huge market of repeat buyers. But if you’re going to make it easy to burn through and not have the inventory to support the captive replacement audience, you’re missing a big opportunity.  Shame.  Another big Nike release with a short term focus getting in its own way

Lighten up on the upper. First some positives. The Vomero looks fantastic. When this shoe has reached the end of its running life, it will fit in great as a street wear shoe, which I can't say for many of my running shoes. It's well cushioned, but you already knew that. It's also very stable. And the waffle tread on the outsole provides fantastic grip. The downsides are all related. The Vomero is extremely plush, heavy, and warm. First the plush - the upper is downright pillowy. When you lace up, the tongue just spills out, swallowing the laces like a pillow. It's really impossible to overstate how thick and plush the tongue and the rest of the upper is on this shoe. All that pillowy softness adds weight, which is noticeable, especially on long runs. And, as you might expect, breathability is terrible. There's essentially no airflow and your feet will feel really warm unless it's cold outside. I also own a pair of Nike Invincible 3's. The run is just as cushioned (and a bit unstable) with a massive slab of ZoomX foam underfoot. But the upper on the Invincibles is engineered for breathability and weight savings. I don't mind a heavy shoe where the weight is due to the foam underfoot. For me, though, the weight isn't worth it if that weight comes from a plush upper - especially with the problems of breathability. Switching to an engineered, lightweight upper would be a massive improvement. As-is, the Vomero is hard to recommend

Suggestion Nike: Create a low price racing Vomero with a curved carbon fiber or nylon insert and watch the revenue explode. In fact call it the VOMERO EXPLODE! This shoe is simple enough to help anyone train without strain and be ready for the big race, but it may also keep competitors up at night!

Max Cushioning for real. They really do mean max cushioning wow. My first run with them was entirely knee pain free. i felt supported and cushioned on every stride . i don’t run for performance or speed, at the moment i just do it as a fun way to keep fit. If you’re looking for max speed look elsewhere, if you want comfort and support during your long runs, these are defo the shoe for you. I also don’t feel like they’re too heavy which is the main issue i see with other reviewers. they’re also not too hot. Honestly to me they do exactly what they advertise and they do it well. Vomero 18s gained a new fan. i also love the red orange and pink sunset design. just a fantastic all-round shoe!

These are my new go-tos for everyday, longer runs!. I am obsessed with the amount of support in these! The Vomeros 18’s are the softest and tallest (stack height) pair of running shoes I’ve worn- the exaggerated rocker shape with the higher toe spring takes the impact off my knees, allowing me to run longer and easier miles without any aches or pains which I love. Next-Level Cushioning. I was excited to try the new Vomero 18, and it truly did not disappoint! This version takes everything great about the Vomero line and makes it even better, especially with the introduction of the dual layers of foam. The taller stack height provides an extra layer of plush but responsive cushioning making them feel incredibly soft and smooth but without sacrificing stability. The ZoomX foam layer on top gives that springy, energetic bounce while the ReactX foam added underneath provides a stable and durable base. This combo gives the shoe a smooth ride whether I'm going for a recovery jog but still has the energy to keep up and push me on those longer runs. Overall, for everyday runners who prioritize comfort, cushioning and a bit of extra bounce the Vomero 18 is a fantastic pick! Vomero 18. The Nike Vomero 18 is a continued improvement on a long running Vomero line. The Vomero 18 features a lightweight mesh upper and a thick cushioning “Zoom X” sole. I would recommend trying these on if you can. Also, the thick sole can be hard to adjust to at first due. I almost tripped over them as they make me about half an inch taller.  The Zoom X sole paired with the gradient color of the midsole really makes the design pop. I love wearing these to the gym for running, but they look so cool I want to wear them everywhere! Walking on clouds. These shoes are incredibly comfortable for their intended purpose, whether you’re lounging at home or out and about.  When I first put them on, I felt like I was walking on clouds. A few flaws, but gets the job done. The first thing that caught my attention was the unique colorway, which is different than what I'm used to. Conversely, the shoe feels a bit heavy, even with how comfortable it is, which could be a hindrance during long walks or runs. It might not be an issue for some, depending on how they use them, but for others, it could be a potential dealbreaker. If there is one thing in my mind that's a standout quality of this sneaker it's the durability in how it holds up considerably during my long runs and during running errands as well without any discomfort or pain.  Overall, it's a pretty good running shoe if you're willing to overlook some flaws, like its heaviness. It's a few tweaks away from becoming great, and I will continue to use them in the future with the hope that my opinion changes over time

New favorite shoes. The moment I opened the box for these shoes I fell in love. They are so cute and perfect for spring. The bright pink and orange just feel like sunshine and happiness to me. Walking on Clouds. I had a long errand day and I put these shoes to the test and I was pleased. I am a person who will have to change shoes after a while and I did not have to with these. They are cushiony and comfortable. The inside is soft and they are lightweight. It was almost as if I could forget I was even wearing shoes.   I also like the design. They are a bit more vibrant in color than I usually choose, but I don’t regret the colorwave at all. I love the small details like the silver metallic writing in the toe area.   I initially thought the shoe was too thick at the sole, but I understand the purpose was to pack on the cushion. I am glad they did. Cushion to the Max!. The insole says it all, “Maximum Cushioning.” I took these bad boys out for a 3 mile run and to no surprise with all this cushion, it was a smooth ride! With a higher midsole profile that provides maximum cushion, your feet will thank you. The cushion absorbs all the shock when your heel strikes the ground. The cushion also feels like it recoils and propels you during the launch phase of running. The heel counter is rigid and provides good support so that there’s no sliding side to side.  The mesh upper provides breathability and more comfort. Running in Oregon where it rains 250 days out of the year, I’m hoping and pretty sure Nike will offer a Gore-Tex or Shield version of the Vomero 18.  I wish the traction on the outsole was better. There is a lot of smooth surfaces on the midfoot which kind of scares me when running on wet surfaces. The shoe lacing is definitely different. Instead of your standard loop holes for your lace eyelets, there is a flywire stitched into the eyestay reinforcement panel. I’m curious the longevity of this lace system and I wonder if this will tear over time from consistent use. I would love to see more 3M reflective safety stripes for those early morning or late night runners.  Overall, this is a great shoe for both running and walking. Comfort is a 10 out of 10! I would even recommend it to people who are on their feet all day. I’m just curious of the longevity of the shoe lace system and would like to see more traction!

Summer Vibes Runs. I want to start off with saying that I am obsessed with the color pattern ombré from start to end, I would love to see more neutrals colors for everyday wear! So comfortable! Decent. Squeak / weight. So close to being excellent. Part of my review is practical and part is personal preference -  Practical: squeaks. The shoe squeaks. It’s one of my greatest pet peeves and worries and frustrations with “air” - it doesn’t always happen, but when it does, it’s horrible.  I don’t like noisy or squeaky shoes of any kind - and air can be particularly bad.  In these, only the right shoe squeaks. And it’s every step. A two-beat squeak. It’s awful.  To me, it speaks to the fact that it doesn’t have to be that way (one shoe doesn’t do it) and therefore somewhere the quality was lower in the right shoe.  Usually, with air, it takes a puncture or etc for stuff like that to happen / over time. This immediate squeak just made me sad.   Personal preference: I don’t like a bulky shoe. I prefer slim and athletic - I think these are less bulky than I imagined (and I’m definitely not saying “heavy”) but they’re…thick. And naturally, the midsole and outsole combined are weightier than the upper. It’s difficult for this not to happen in making a shoe, but I don’t want to feel as if I’m lifting / dragging some weight off the ground as the upper holds on tight to it. I want the shoe to feel like one “whole.” I come at this from a soccer players perspective. The footwear should be connected as one. Disconnected is not a good feeling.  Now, runners who run run run a lot may find it to work out well over long distances / many miles. I don’t imagine that being the case, but it may be.   Air has the benefit of not ever really losing the bounce - as foam can compress eventually and then it’s done - but air adds weight and variables such as squeaks - and height which can lead to some roll/slightly less stability (not calling the shoe unstable) -  And I personally don’t love that feeling.   I digress - tho I’m ready to work in Nike design and testing whenever they’re ready to have me :!

These shoes ARE GREAT. These shoes ARE great. In response to any criticism that these “cause your heel to wobble”—shoes do not cause that, rather a lack of stabilizing strength in a person is the cause. Those who struggle with stability should seek out shoes that will help them with their issue instead of blaming a shoe. Those who want comfort and cushion but do not need extra stabilizing support, the combination of air unit, react x, and zoom x are genuinely hard to beat. This is a GREAT shoe for regular mid distance runners, daily walkers, or those just looking to enjoy the pleasure of how great this shoe feels

Best Running Shoe Ever. When the shoes were delivered to me, I didn’t think I had gotten the correct shoes with the box being super light. To my surprise, the shoes were in fact just this light! With the Air all on the bottom of the shoe I was skeptical on how this would perform as a running shoe. After a few runs outside on gravel roads and inside on treadmills, I can confidently say these are the Best Running Shoes I had ever used! This shoe was made for running! The mesh upper and ventilation all through the shoe tongue helps tremendously when running in warm weather. Adding additional comfort to the bottom of the shoe is definitely noticeable having no pain in my legs after running multiple miles! Since I started running in these shoes, I have had a new Personal Record in miles for the new year! I am extremely impressed in not just the fit of the shoe, but the overall support with the shoe being super light! One thing that I really enjoyed about the inside of the shoe was the additional padding around the ankle and heel to give that extra cushion. I decided to go with my true size of size 10 and was happy I did. The upper mesh material does help give a little extra room in case you are looking for additional room in the width area. The only negative is there is no Gore-Tex or weatherized version at this time, but if these are made, I would be one of the first to purchase! If you are looking for a new type of running shoe that not only performs but also looks good while running then the Peg Premium is the shoe for you!

Squeaky Nightmare – Beware of These Shoes!. I had high hopes for these shoes, but unfortunately, they turned out to be one of the most annoying pairs I’ve ever owned. Right out of the box, they looked great—sleek design, comfortable fit—but the moment I started walking, the nightmare began.  These shoes squeak with every single step, no matter what surface I’m on. Tile? Squeak. Hardwood? Squeak. Even on carpet, there’s still a faint noise. It’s like having a built-in sound effect following me everywhere. I’ve tried everything—adjusting the laces, checking the insoles, even applying powder to the soles—but nothing stops the relentless noise.  What’s worse, the squeaking isn’t just a minor annoyance—it’s LOUD. I wore them to work once, and people kept turning to see where the sound was coming from. Walking through a quiet hallway or a library? Forget it. You might as well announce your arrival from a mile away.  Comfort-wise, they’re decent, but honestly, that doesn’t matter when I feel like I’m starring in my own personal sound effects reel. Unless you enjoy the embarrassment of sounding like a human rubber duck, I strongly recommend avoiding these shoes

Clouds on Your Feet!. Overall, these Nike Pegasus Premium Women’s Road Running Shoes deliver a great mix of comfort, style, and performance.   They fit true to size, with a snug yet flexible feel. The cushioning is well-balanced—soft enough for comfort but firm enough to provide good energy return. Nike always nails aesthetics, and these are no exception. The sleek, modern design with classic branding makes them stylish for both workouts and casual wear. The color options are so versatile! The upper mesh fabric is breathable, keeping my feet cool even during longer runs. It also feels durable yet lightweight.  The cushioning and lightweight build were the standout features for me. They provided a smooth ride without feeling too bulky, making them great for both short sprints and long-distance runs. I would love to see a wider toe box, especially for those with wider feet than me.   If you’re looking for a reliable, stylish, and comfortable running shoe, the Nike Pegasus Premium is a great choice
Wow. These are good. These are very good. I walked 6 miles this morning. Zero foot fatigue. I felt like the sneakers weee pushing me forward, even more than I was willing to go. EXTEREMY PLEASED

Great for running! Very soft and a smooth ride. My main running shoe is a pair of Nike Vomero 16s when running on pavement and then I use the Nike Ultrafly for trail running. This is an excellent upgrade for those that want extreme cushioning when running on pavement but I don't think this is a good shoe for more rugged trails if you're thinking about that.   The feeling underfoot is very squishy and bouncy and you feel like you will propel forward when striking and hitting the air unit beneath the zoomx foam. Pair that with the reactx foam and you have a very comfortable but heavy pair of shoes when running. Weight can be a con for some people and I will say that this shoe does feel heavy when on your feet so if you're looking for a lighter shoe this will not be the shoe for you.  However when running it does feel nice as a mid foot and sometime forefoot striker, for heel strikers it seems like it'll be a smooth transition as well.  Overall I do enjoy the shoe and will be using these on my future runs!

Better than Anticipated. The Pegasus Premium Running Shoes are solid. They feel well made, durable, and look great. Men’s Pegasus Premium Road Running Shoes are Top Tier! 1000/10. I am so thrilled that I was able to cop the newest Pegasus running shoe - the Pegasus Premium! I got these for running, and they truly are PREMIUM. Premium quality, comfort, weight, and features!   These shoes are so lightweight and bouncy! I feel like I’m 20 pounds lighter and feel so limber as I run. The air at the bottom of the shoe makes it so comfortable and makes it feel like I’m walking or running on a bouncy cloud.  The look of the shoe is fantastic. They are just so cool - I love the look of the air at the bottom, the large swoosh on the sides, and  the inner sides of the shoes that say “zoom” and “air”. There are reflective elements to the shoe too which make it great for running at night or in low visibility conditions.   The only thing I didn’t love about the shoe is the tongue. It’s definitely comfortable, but it has a bunch of small holes all over the top part of the tongue, closer to the toes. I’m sure they’re there for breathability, but not my favorite look.  I would absolutely recommend to a friend - these are a game changer for my running practices!! So fire!

The fabric is soft, breathable, and durable, providing a great balance of comfort and longevity. Overall, a fantastic pair of shoes that offer style, comfort, and performance!

Bouncy Premium Running Shoe. The Nike Pegasus Premium Men's Road Running Shoes is an amazing running shoe. It's super comfortable, has amazing shock absorption, and a great "bounce" to keep you onto the next step. True to size fit for me and it was perfect, no shifting at all even when running. Materials feel pretty high quality and help with the overall feel and comfort of the shoe. The air and and foam sole combo creates a sole that provides all day comfort. The design reminds me of an Air Max 97 with a more breathable upper that's soft and better suited for running and jogging. I loved the amount of bounce the sole provided. The sole did feel like it added a bit of height, which I didn't mind. Also with the amount of bounce, it did take a tiny bit of time to get used to the feeling while walking, but it was very good for running. Overall, I think these are the best running shoes I've ever had. Super comfy and stylish

Walking on Clouds. If you're on the hunt for sneakers that prioritize serious comfort, look no further than the Nike Pegasus.   From the moment I slipped these on, it felt like my feet were being cradled by fluffy clouds. I know this sounds cliche, but try it and see for yourself!   The cushioning is incredibly plush, making each step feel amazing. Whether you're in for a morning run, exploring a new city, or simply want to treat your feet to some TLC, these sneakers deliver. I've worn them for hours without any hint of discomfort or fatigue. Additionally, these are super airy, which means no sweating in these! The Air Magic technology  truly makes a difference.  Pros: *Unbelievable cushioning for all-day comfort *Spacious and super airy *Stylish design that doesn't compromise on comfort  Cons: *I got them in white, and these are a super bright white which makes it stand out in my opinion. I would have preferred a softer white color. Bottom Line:  The Nike Pegasus is a game-changer for anyone who values comfort above all else. Say goodbye to aching feet and hello to a walking-on-clouds experience

Lots of Cushion. The Nike Pegasus Premium is a very very comfortable running shoe. The Zoomx foam provides a great amount of cushion that feels like you're walking/running on a cloud. The circular knit upper causes great breathability for your feet on those long runs but it is not ideal for those wet conditions. I've primarily used it for shorter runs and the design of the shoe really propels your momentum forward which helps when you start getting tired. It motivated me to pick up the pace. The aesthetics of the Pegasus Premium in this color way is really nice in person.  I like how far the Pegasus running shoe has developed throughout the years. I had the Pegasus 34 back in 2018 and this Pegasus Premium has advanced in comfort, design, and technology. The waffle outsole provides good traction and extends the life of the outsole if you heel drag. Shoe runs true to size. I normally do a half size up for running shoes but I did not have to in this case. If the Nike Vaporfly recommended for long distances like marathon's the Nike Pegasus Premium is for everything in between

The Future of Nike!. These shoes are what made Nike, Nike! Full length zoom on top of multiple foams. The cushion is like the Hulk on steroids. Super comfortable, super innovative! I’ve been getting compliments about it every time I wear them. Nike is pushing the limits with this one! The only thing I don’t like, is that it’s narrow by the midfoot. I suspect this will loosen up over time because this is a running shoe and it needs time to break in. Not the cushioning, that’s super soft out of the box!!

Loved these and would buy them again!. As someone who appreciates a morning jog, I was very excited to try these Nike Pegasus Premium Road running shoes. I tend to be uncomfortable in shoes when I’m running. All the shoes I’ve tried in the past seemed like shoes to me, until I tried these. When I put these on for the first time, I immediately loved them. They actually feel like they are aiding in my run. They help my body stay comfortable for the run. There are so many holes in these shoes, I am able to get plenty of air to my feet, helping keep them dry. There is still enough fabric on the shoes, however, to keep my feet dry and clean from any puddles or dirt I encounter. The fabric itself felt nice and resistant, like it would be easy to get out stains and keep clean, and wouldn’t rip easily. The foam insoles keep the balls of my feeling from aching. It is a comfortable amount of padding without feeling like you’re sinking into them. The bottoms of the shoe have an amazing layer of springs. When running, this gives me a major boost that helps keep my knees high, and ease them from pain. This feature is by far my favorite part about these shoes. They are also highly reflective, so I feel safe running in them at night, especially when crossing the street. The fit of these shoes was nice. I ordered a size 7, and they were very true to size. The only change I would make to these shoes, is making the springs on the bottom a tiny bit wider in the middle. I would definitely buy another pair of these shoes, and would highly recommend them to others

Great shoes! Just a little small. These Nike running sneakers definitely have a comfortable fit. They do however run a little small. I am a women’s size 9 and that’s what I got. They are just a little bit too tight on top in the center. I would  recommend going a size up if you have a wider foot like me or if you are someone who is 1 size, but sometimes goes bigger. Besides them being a little too snug, they are comfortable and they feel good and sturdy on my feet.   The design is out of this world. Think of cool and classy. They are the perfect sleek white sneakers with a beautiful pop of neon orange here and there, making me want to go on runs all the time. The Nike swoosh looks great on the side and top! And I love how the whole name of the shoe is written on the top in orange. They make me feel like I am running and walking in style!  The fabric of the shoe is very nice. It is soft and feels comfortable. It has breathable mesh, but it is not too cold for the winter. I’ve been wearing them around this winter and they keep my feet warm! You don’t have to wear these just for runs and walks. They are also perfect for going around shopping when you are doing a lot of walking or just going out to grab coffee. The bottom of the shoe is thicker with a lot of spring which makes them feel lightweight and quick. They definitely help quicken your step without doing as much work.   Overall I loved the style, the fabric, the mechanics of the shoe, but I gave it a 4 star because they were just a little small. Although they were a little small, that won’t keep me from recommending them! I’d recommend these to anyone who loves running, walking, shopping, and just going out and about in style in practical shoes!

The most fun shoes I’ve ever worn!. Fit and feel - I’ll start off by saying I think these fit true to size and they feel AMAZING! Some of the previous Pegasus models seem to run a bit long in my experience but these fit just right. They’re SUPER bouncy. I can’t emphasize that enough. For lack of a better explanation, these feel fun to wear! The ZoomX foam is very responsive and cushy. The ReactX is responsive as well, but a bit more firm which adds some stability which is always a plus. The standout feature is the exposed full-length Air Zoom unit though. It is super responsive and bouncy. It really feels like the shoe is doing the work for you while running.    Design - the overall design is really nice and innovative. The exposed Zoom Air unit is really good looking and stands out big time! The shape is really functional with the heel being higher than the toe creating a rocker-like shape to help propel you forward. I love how the orange contrasts against the white/grey upper. It really pops and stands out on this shoe. Overall it’s a really nice shoe that performs just as good as it looks!   Materials - the materials used on this shoe are very functional. There’s a lot of ventilated mesh on the upper which cuts down on weight and helps with breathability. The knit on the toe box is super breathable as well. I didn’t notice my feet getting hot in these at all during casual wear or while running in them. The reflective piping around the shoe and the reflective accents on the laces are nice touches in case you run in low light conditions. The tongue is nice and padded but has some cutouts for added ventilation. The ankle collar is also very well padded and I didn’t notice any heel slippage because of this while running. The shoes feel durable while also remaining flexible, breathable and pretty lightweight overall.   Pros/cons - to be quite honest I can’t think of any cons. They fit great, they’re one of the most bouncy/responsive shoes I’ve ever worn, they look great and they make running feel more fun and less like exercise for me personally! I love these shoes and I’m really looking forward to putting more miles on them!

Walking on clouds!. These shoes literally feel like you are walking on clouds! They are extremely comfortable and give support everywhere you need it. They have  mesh fabric that makes them breathable which is great for people like me that have sweaty feet 24/7. The design is beautiful. The way they have the support and comfort you want along with a style that still makes them attractive shoes. I do personally feel like they run a tad bit small so if you’re debating on sizing up I would definitely do that. These shoes also give you a good couple inches in height. If you are a short king wanting to increase your height or a shorter woman wanted to appear taller these will give you that boost naturally without it being overly obvious. Overall, great shoes love everything about them hoping they will have even more color combos so I can get every pair they have!!!!!!

Bouncy Fit. My first thought putting on the Nike Pegasus was wow, these are bouncy. It feels like you are walking on clouds. It honestly takes a bit to get used to since it’s not like your normal shoes. It definitely promotes a runners gate where your foot rolls to the ball of your feet as you move. If you struggle with your feet hitting hard on the ground as you run, these would help! The fabric is really breathable too, which is nice. I’m a true 8.5 and as always, it seems Nike runs a touch small. While I can fit in them, I do feel my toe is touching right up to the edge of the shoe and I would prefer a 9 for better comfort.   Overall I like the shoe for running due to the squishy sole design that keeps your feet comfortable and moving from heel to toe. However, I would not recommend for an everyday shoe as the shoe slants such that you stay more on the ball of your feet

Excellent ground cushion. These shoes fit true to size and the ground cushioning is like walking on air. Great impact resistance for walking, running or standing on hard surfaces. The only thing I didn't enjoy is the top materials holes looked somewhat tacky and definitely would let in moisture if walking through wet surfaces. Could be more padding around the ankle. But this was the softest step I've ever experienced

Zoom In, Zoom Out. I was really excited to give these a go when I saw just how stacked they were with cushioning, they don't disappoint. In hand and on feet they're substantially light, and just casually walking around is almost effortless with how much response they give underfoot.  They do require you to pay a little extra attention when you first put them on, as they don't play around when it comes to layering on the midsole. But after a minute or so they adapted pretty well. Once I got to take them out on a casual run they were like a dream. So much energy return, it was a fun feeling I haven't had in a while. All that zoom air underfoot made just bouncing around with every stride into a total breeze. And the lightweight knit upper helps keep things cool too, which is thankfully pretty standard nowadays. My favorite feature has to be the tongue, with all the perforations it especially keeps things cool, and surprisingly keeps structural integrity pretty in tact.  That being said, they struggled a bit with me personally when it came to serious running. I always strike with my forefoot instead of my heel when actually running and not just jogging around. Even though the zoom air is full length, they're clearly meant for you to crash onto the heel portion for maximum responsiveness. They're certainly still very good for running, just not as great as having more substantial dedicated zoom air pods in the forefoot, like with some of Nike's other competition running shoes. Fore those that rely on their heels for crashing, these will suit you excellently. Even with this, I was still able to log well over a dozen miles, with hundreds more in store
Great shoe l!!!!. I got these about a week ago and they are one of my best running shoes. There overall peformence is amazing!!! If you are in to running and want a great shoe then this is for you!

Best Running Shoes I’ve Ever Owned. I think these are the most comfortable running shoes I’ve ever owned. My calf muscles feel really relaxed in the shoe, and I find the design to be very unique and unlike any shoe I’ve ever owned. They definitely stand out in my collection and give me a pop cold color when I want to switch it up. I really love the orange color way. The shoes fit well, but after being in them for a few hours they start to feel a little smaller because the air bubble doesn’t go all the way around the base of the shoe. If I had to make a change I would want the shoes to have a wider base as my feet typically run wide.  The fabric is very clean and flexible and has a sleek design. I honestly love every part of the shoe, but if I had to pick a  favorite part it would be the overall design

Great Fit & Comfort! Stylish!. These Nike Pegasus Road Running shoes are SO comfortable. They have a very cushioned sole (hence the name Air) and are very breathable mesh on top. I’m 34 and I feel like these make me look cooler, but I don’t look like I’m desperately trying to pretend I’m 21. They’re a great balance of style and function while not being too flashy. I sometimes struggle with shoes having too narrow of a toe box and these have plenty of room for me to flex my toes. I also sometimes struggle with tying shoes and then having arch pain. These laces can extend pretty far up to the base of my ankle providing supporting without being overly tight on my arch. I’m not normally a big orange girly but these are so cute and I will definitely be using them as my new running shoes!

Bouncy and Light. When I put these on my feet for the first time, I immediately wanted to get out of the house and put them to work. The fit was great right out of the box and I was shocked at how much bounce I could feel. These put a spring in every step you take. The design is really cool and I quite like the inclusion of reflective material and the look of the full-length Air Zoom unit in the sole, which also gives a nice color pop. The fabric is knitted in such a way that you can see through it on some parts of the shoe, which makes it very breathable. Despite being so breathable, I like how the shoe still manages to feel structured and keep its shape. If I had to choose my favorite thing about the Nike Pegasus Premium, it would be how responsive they feel - which is more than any shoe I've worn before. If I could change one very minor thing, I'd make the pull tab on the tongue a little different, because when I was tying the laces I wasn't sure if they're supposed to go under or over it. To sum up all my thoughts, I'd say these are simply great

Headed in the Right Direction. Nike has taken everything great about the Pegasus design and elevated it with new features—without overcomplicating things.  Lifestyle Wear: A Perfect Everyday Shoe As a lifestyle shoe, the Pegasus Premium is an excellent choice.  With a full-length ZoomX midsole, Air Zoom layers, and a ReactX heel, it offers outstanding comfort for all-day wear. Whether you're walking around on vacation, standing at work, or simply looking for a versatile everyday sneaker, this shoe delivers.  With more colorways on the way, I highly recommend visiting a store to try them on for yourself.  Training & Gym Performance: Almost There When it comes to training, the Pegasus Premium is close—but not quite perfect.  I do a mix of high-intensity training and traditional weightlifting, so I prefer shoes that are lightweight and low to the ground. I like to feel a strong connection through my feet when I train.  I tested the Pegasus Premium with and without my custom insoles:  With my custom insoles – The added support created too much bounce, making movements like running, jumping, and force generation uncomfortable. It felt like my foot was on a rounded surface. Without my custom insoles – The built-in ZoomX and React cushioning still felt too soft for high-intensity workouts. While great for comfort, it made it hard to feel the ground when running or training. If you don’t do much cardio, sprinting, or explosive movements, this shoe works fine for the gym. However, if you're used to lightweight trainers, you’ll likely notice the extra weight.  That said, I do appreciate Nike moving away from the pointed heel design found in previous Pegasus models—I was never a fan of that look, so it’s great to see them refining it.  Final Thoughts & Recommendation The Pegasus Premium is a solid shoe, but whether it's right for you depends on your needs.  For everyday wear? Absolutely. For running and training? It depends on your preference. The extra cushioning and weight might not work for those who prefer a more grounded feel.  At its premium price point, I strongly recommend trying them on in person before purchasing. Overall, Nike is heading in the right direction, and with more feedback from the community, they have the potential to refine this model into something even better

BETTER THAN EXPECTED. The Pegasus Premium provides great cushioning, comfort, and durability. The upper fits well and has nice padding around the ankle. I also love how it is designed to be lightweight and breathable. I like how the mesh material feels supported all around too. Overall great quality shoe for your everyday running or even walking!

Nike Pegasus Premium Review. Fit/Sizing: Runs true to size with a secure and comfortable fit that adapts well to different foot shapes like my wide feet.   Materials: Breathable mesh upper with plush and responsive cushioning for a smooth ride.  This shoe is built for runners and everyday wear, offering a nice mix of softness and bounce. It feels light and each step feels effortless. The traction holds up well on different surfaces like trails and tracks. The overall design gives a sleek and modern look.  Whether you’re logging miles or just on your feet all day, this shoe delivers on both comfort and performance. It’s a solid pick if you want something cushioned, responsive, and stylish

Wow Factor Running Shoes. I was incredibly impressed with the look of these shoes right out of the box! They definitely have that wow factor in terms of design. The neutral grey makes the bright orange air bubble that goes the length of the shoe really pop. Another really nice design and functional feature is the reflective strip that goes around the whole shoe. I receive tons of compliments when I wear them.    The fabric is perfect for a running shoe. The top is really thin and breathable in a good way. Although it’s winter right now and they feel good with regular socks, these would be ideal for warm weather as the thin, breathable fabric and well placed vents would really keep your foot cool.   The feel of these shoes is amazing just like the rest of the shoe. It feels like you are walking/running on air, literally. They have a really nice spring and bounce to them as you walk/jog/run. It also feels like the air bubble conforms to your foot, even under your toes. They are not overly structured which I like for more casual wear, but I would think about using a more structured insole for my distance running which I do with most of my running shoes for better arch support and to keep my foot in the right position. I’ve worn these for 13 hours straight and they were extremely comfortable the whole time.  As far as fit, I would say these run a little small. I got my true size which is fine for casual wear and light running or workouts but for running any distance, I would probably size up a half or even a full size. Also, I felt like the shoe tips your foot forward so naturally your foot slides forward a bit which is another reason I would probably size up at least a half size for consistent running or distance running.   There are so many really fantastic features about this shoe, but my favorite feature both for the looks and the comfort factor is probably the full length air bubble that extends the length of the shoe. I love the way it looks and feels. Second to that is the reflective strip which surprised me but it is so functional and really stands out in low light conditions. I am constantly finishing up a run or outing with my kids in the evening when the increased visibility is really helpful.   The only thing I would change about this shoe based on my personal preference is a little more structured support in the insole and I would order up a half size.   Overall, this is an amazing shoe! I would highly recommend for anything from a pair of really good looking casual shoes or for more intense running or training. You’ll definitely get noticed when you go out in this shoe!

Spring in Your Step. I'm always a bright color person, so from the moment I opened the shoe box I was happy with the style. That orange strip across the bottom really pops against that white. However, because the base color is white, it goes with almost all of my exercise clothing which is always a plus!  The lacing on these shoes allow for a tighter or more relaxed fit. I have wider feet so I'm pretty particular about the fit. I was able to tighten them down without sacrificing comfort.  I'm not normally much of a street runner, as I prefer to be out on trails. However, these shoes are definitely better for street running than trail running. The breathability is excellent, and I anticipate it to be even better when summer comes. When using these shoes on trails, the breathability is actually problematic as it lets all of the fine sandstone dirt right in. I end up having to stop and empty my shoes a few times each run.  When hiking, the mesh isn't as irritating, as the dirt isn't being actively kicked up. Because of my preference being out on trails, I'm only giving it a four-stars.  The technology on the back of the heel is really nice. It allows me to slide my foot in pretty seamlessly. This decreases the time needed to get ready to hit the trail. As a busy mom, every second counts when trying to get that exercise in.   Now to get to the Crown Jewel of these shoes. The technology used on the bottom of the shoes is unmatched for comfort. I have worn many different brands and styles, old and upcoming. The spring that these shoes give to each step is pretty amazing. It almost feels like my shoes are working with me when I'm running instead of just being protectors to my feet. It feels like they are also protecting my joints. The shoe is firm and supportive while responding to each and every movement habit you have. It compresses as you step  down and bounces back up assisting with every stride. Comfort of a cloud but responsiveness of a spring.  Overall, if you are mainly exercising off of a dirt trail, these shoes will serve you well. The breathability, responsiveness, fit, and comfort are top-notch. The only downside is the mesh, and it will only hinder you if you're running on dirt

Sleek shoes that performs with a couple caveats. Nike Pegasus like running on springy clouds. The Nike Pegasus shoes got me back into regular running. Pegasus Premium Running Shoes. The Nike Pegasus Premium Running Shoes are a fantastic blend of thoughtful design, comfort, and performance. What makes them stand out is their attention to detail and homage to Nike’s rich history.  One of my favorite aspects of these shoes is the small details that pay tribute to Nike’s past, present, and future. On the upper part of the shoe, you’ll find the coordinates to the Santa Monica Blue Ribbon Sports building, where Nike’s journey began. This building, once the heart of Nike’s origin story, now serves as a community space for runners. Right next to the coordinates, the inscription “BRS est. 1967” nods to Nike’s original name, Blue Ribbon Sports. The heel tabs feature Pegasus wings, a perfect symbol for these shoes, as they truly give you a sense of "flying" when running. The combination of advanced materials makes these shoes a joy to run in. The Zoom X foam, Air Zoom, and React X foam deliver exceptional comfort and responsiveness. They feel like they propel you forward with every step, making running feel almost effortless. The upper material is highly breathable, keeping your feet cool and reducing moisture during long runs. The shoe has reflective laces and a strip around the shoe enhance visibility, making them a great choice for early morning or nighttime runs.  With their thoughtful details, top-tier comfort, and excellent performance, the Nike Pegasus Running Premium Shoes are a standout choice for runners. Whether you’re logging miles or running errands, they offer a perfect balance of style and functionality

Comfort & Style!. I'm really impressed with the Nike Pegasus Premium Women's Road Running Shoes! I'm someone who lives in athleisure but also goes to the gym, likes to run outside and stay active. The Fun Run. The Nike Pegasus Premium is a game-changer for the streets. From the moment I unboxed it, the bold orange design caught my eye—it’s sleek, modern, and turns heads without being too loud. The breathable mesh upper isn’t just for show either; it keeps your feet cool, even when you’re pounding the pavement in the heat.  The air bubble tech hits different. It’s like walking on pillows that bounce back with every step. Once you lace up and hit the ground running, you’ll notice how the foam molds to your foot, giving you that snug, custom-fit feel. On my first mile, the Pegasus stayed locked in—no slipping, no rubbing, just smooth strides. And the ankle support? Top-tier. It’s like having extra security for those sharp turns or unexpected dips in the sidewalk.  Even after the run, I felt fresh—no soreness or awkward pressure points. The only downside is  a little squeak from the foam after the workout, but honestly, it’s a small trade-off for how good this shoe is overall.  This shoe doesn’t just perform—it embodies Nike as a brand. From its innovative design to its focus on comfort and performance, the Pegasus Premium is a reflection of everything Nike stands for: pushing boundaries, setting standards, and delivering excellence.  For runners of all levels—whether you’re in the gym killing it on the treadmill or just jogging through the city—the Nike Pegasus Premium delivers. It’s not just a running shoe; it’s a statement. Comfortable, supportive, and stylish, this is a must-have for anyone looking to level up their sneaker game while repping the best in the business

Great balance for sure. Upon opening the box, the first thing I noticed was the vibrant bright orange color along the sole, and I was immediately sold—orange is my favorite color! There is also a lightweight foam layer near the sole that I'm curious about in terms of durability. This sneaker definitely serves as a fashion statement, but it can also function as a running shoe. I would choose to wear it for style, but it seems capable of handling a run as well. They feel very light on my feet, which is a plus. I enjoy wearing it, but I’m not a fan of the thin toe material

My favorite  shoes. I absolutely love these shoes for their unmatched comfort and style. Super comfy Pegasus shoe!. As someone who already has and loves the Pegasus 41, these Pegasus Premiums blew me away. Ladies - Do NOT Miss Out!. Ladies! I have honestly finally found the perfect running/training shoe! I have always been a Nike girl, and I have enjoyed many of their products over the years, but the Pegasus Premium is the cream of the crop! Mom of 3 - AMAZING shoe. Let me start off with this, I am extremely picky with my shoes as I have a club foot. Comfortability is everything for me as that makes it a little challenging finding the "perfect" shoe.  I am normally a Women's Size 7 but I did get a 7.5 and they fit just right. The Nike React foam was a total game-changer, as soon as I put these on and walked around, it was like walking on a cloud. The shoe has breathable mesh  and great traction for any elements. A really comfortable shoe. This is my first time trying a pair of premium sneakers. I was so excited first time I stood and walked around in the Nike Pegasus Premium shoes.  I was really surprised at how incredibly cushioned, bouncy, and supported my feel were inside such an airy lightweight shoe. I’m a 54 year-old former ballerina, and my feet are pretty beat up. These padding and support in the Pegasus Premium makes my feet feel like I could walk miles without pain.   I really like the pale gray-white color and the orange air cushion accent. The gray laces look nice against the shoe, and the orange tips give a great pop of color. I don’t like the slick nylon texture of the laces. They were slippery when I tied the shoes, and I don’t know how long they will make it before needing retied.   The neutral colors of the shoes is great. A neutral color scheme means I can wear these shoes for exercise, going out, and with casual outfits.   The material of the upper is mesh-like and very breathable. This will be great for Texas summers, when my feet hate being in hot shoes. The drawback with mesh is the mesh allows in sand, grit, pebbles and debris. If the weather is wet, you have wet feet and socks in a mesh shoe. These are great dry weather shoes for sure.   The Pegasus Premium soles have great traction. We had an unprecedented snow storm yesterday, and everything is icy outside. I took a quick walk and didn’t slip once. These soles offered great traction and a good grip on uneven, slippery terrain.   I was surprised a little by the shoe’s fit. They seem to run a little small. I usually wear a US 6.5, but I’m wearing a 7.5 in the Pegasus Premium to get my usual fit. I have wide toes, and many athletic shoes are too narrow. The Pegasus Premium is the perfect width for my feet, without having to size up to the wide width shoe.   Overall I really like the Nike Pegasus Premium. It is very comfortable, looks great, and fits my style

Amazing Running Shoes. The Nike Pegasus premium running shoes are nothing short of amazing! From the second I put them on I loved the way they felt! My favorite feature would be how comfortable they are and how easy they were to try on and break them in with a run! Awesome recovery shoe.. I have really enjoyed running in the Nike Vomero Plus. The cushioning is the standout feature. It feels plush and protective without being unstable, which makes a big difference on longer runs and recovery days. My legs feel noticeably fresher after higher mileage sessions.  The ride is smooth and consistent, especially at easy to steady paces. Heel to toe transitions feel controlled, and the shoe gives me confidence on pavement when I am settling into a rhythm. The upper is comfortable and breathable, with enough room in the forefoot to stay comfortable as miles add up.  This is not a speed focused shoe, and it does feel heavier when I try to push the pace. For tempo runs or faster efforts, I would reach for something lighter. But for daily training, long runs, and steady aerobic work, it does exactly what I want.  Overall, the Vomero Plus is a dependable, comfort driven running shoe that excels at protecting my legs and keeping runs enjoyable. It is a great choice for runners who value cushioning, stability, and consistency over pure speed

Great Max Cushion Shoe. Preparing for a marathon and needed a new pair of shoes for my long easy runs. I was a BIG fan of the ZoomX Invincible 1, a slightly lesser fan of the ZI2, and didn’t like the ZI3 at all (not a bad shoe, just wasn’t for me). I switched to Brooks for my last marathon and they didn’t hold up well, which put me back in the market. When I read about the Vomero Plus picking up where the Invincible line left off, I was intrigued. I have to say, 126 miles later, this is an awesome max cushion shoe. Combines the best elements of the Invincible line with key improvements, particularly the upper and the outsole. Also true to size (whereas I used to order 1/2 size up). My legs and feet feel incredible after long slow runs and even picking up speed due to the surprising energy return for a max cushion. I am definitely buying another pair when these retire, hopefully around 300 miles. FYI I rotate the Zoom Fly 6 for shorter, faster runs and it seems to be a good combination. I’m not a competitive runner so I didn’t need to buy the more expensive models

Finished 1st 10K Run!. I'm 5'10, around 197 pounds, and I'm in my forties. And I have been training as a runner for a couple of years now. For most of this year, I have been using the Pegasus 41 for daily runs, speed runs, and 5K runs. I also have the Vaporfly 3, which I use exclusively in organized racing. While I am pleased with those shoes in most situations, I felt like I needed a shoe that will help me run longer and farther. And I don't want to wear out the Vaporflys too early. The Vomero Plus is the shoe that helps me in those regards. I was able to have an uninterrupted run for about 80 minutes. And I was able to run for 10,000 meters for the first time. Mind you, the shoe doesn't feel particularly fast. However, it took longer for my feet to get tired while wearing the Vomero Plus, thanks to the generous amount of ZoomX cushioning. I'm quite sure that I had more fun while wearing the Vomero Plus. I just threw on my headphones and ran while listening to a 60-minute podcast episode. Also, this shoe is a little wider in the midfoot than the Peg 41. At the same time, my heel felt pretty stable. And I appreciate how there is more rubber on the outsole than the Peg 41, so the grip is nice. I plan on rotating all three shoes for different goals. But as of today, the Vomero Plus just could be one of my favorite running shoes of all time!

They also cope with picking up the pace, and respond well to tempo/half marathon pace intervals. Fit true to size, with good room in the toe box.  And the bad.  I've taken a star off as the sole is already showing signs of serious wear on the heel after only 75 miles running on flat pavement/road, so don't have much hope of getting to 250. Comfortable shoe to build miles during the week and long runs. I’ve run about 90 miles in these over the last 18 days and I would describe the difference in feel, compared to running in Nike Frees (which I also love) being like going from sleeping on a pad on the floor to sleeping on a memory foam mattress. I’m increasing mileage now as I build up for my next marathon and also wanted something that would help alleviate tension around both Achilles and switching to these shoes seems to help with that. These feel like they are going to help run a ton of miles without wearing down. They do have a little bit of a bounce/wobble to them, but I ultimately like this. I also like it that they don’t have a flyplate, it’s great to have this cushioning without that same amount of forward propulsion for training. One thing to note, the offset is 10mm and I normally run in 8mm or less. I was hesitant to get something with a greater heel to toe drop because I tried that once and it felt like running with a door jam under foot, like blocking my heel from dropping normally. Thankfully, these squish enough when you land that the extra 2mm compared to what I’m used to is not noticeable at all. Finally, while I like these a lot, I would be very curious to try these with more of a Flyknit upper because the upper does feel like a bit of extra cushion and it takes some attention to get the laces tied up tight so that they don’t feel too big or loose. I just saw the overview of the Vomero Premium before I wrote this, and it looks like the new option is a little bit trimmed down in the upper, which looks like it will be great

The ZoomX midsole enables you to push forward, and it's soft on every landing. There is no harshness on landing and nary an instability.    I ought to run at Golden Gate Park with this pair. I hope to push that 5-mile barrier. My goal is to reach 10 miles and keep it consistent.   Does it have any faults or flaws? The laces are too short for me to use the heel lock/runner's loop. I wish the laces were a little longer so I could do that lacing. So, just one more thing that Nike needs to work on is all their running shoes, giving them a roomier toe box

I put these one (I got the white and teal, sooo pretty!) and instantly fell in love.  I feel like they are less clunky than the regular vomeros even though they have more cushion, I didn’t notice the heel thickness but I did notice the return when I run.
