Oh should mention these have great traction and work great on the peloton treadmill (not the plus).    Highly recommend for endurance runs, interval running, everyday walking or walking workouts, etc. they are just great!   If even wear them on half’s but usually for races I’d wear my vapors

Very cushioned feel. I have been wearing these sneakers for running outdoors. I mainly run on the street/concrete and run 2-3 miles a day. They have a really unique feel - it definitely feels like I’m running on an indoor treadmill vs the road with how cushioned they are. Almost feels like they have springs in them as well. Since running can be hard on your body I’m always looking for an extremely supportive and cushioned sneaker and these are definitely both of this things. The fit was true to size, my foot is also wide so sometimes I have trouble with sneakers being too tight on the sides of my foot, but no issue with these. The quality of the fabric was good as well, I wear low socks and have had no blisters or discomfort. The only thing I didn’t love was that they felt a bit heavier than other sneakers and they did make a bit of a louder sound when hitting the ground. Definitely not the quietest sneaker I have run in. Next time I would likely order a more neutral color as well since the pink I got is very bright. Overall the sneakers and very supportive and make running feel low impact

Nike Vomero +. The Nike Vomero Plus delivers exceptional comfort, support, and performance, with plush cushioning that makes every step feel effortless and a smooth, responsive ride perfect for long runs or all-day wear. The only reason I gave it a 9 out of 10 is that the color was a bit brighter than expected otherwise, it’s flawless

Rockstar shoe!. I am always looking for the next best running and everyday shoe. Nike hit it out of the park with this pair of shoes. I run primarily indoor, but some outdoor, as well. This shoe is also great for everyday use. They are lightweight, durable, supportive and trendy.   The feel is amazing. There is so much support and softness. It feels like you’re walking on a pillow, as opposed to, the hard road. The fit is true to size. I have wide feet and did not have  any issues with this pair of shoes.   The design is not something I would usually gravitate towards, but I do have to say I like the way these look on. I was unsure when they came in the mail, but it grew on me. The color is amazing! I especially appreciate the brightness for my outdoor runs. You can’t miss me now!   The fabric is very durable, but soft. My feet tend to get very hot in shoes, but I didn’t have any issues. I felt that there was a lot of give and lightness to the fabric.   It’s too hard to pick one thing I love about these shoes. I’d have to say the color is great and I really like the feel of the soles. It felt like I could jump on them like I would on a trampoline. So soft and comforting for my feet! If I could change one thing I’d like to have them look less “bulky”. The shoes are lightweight, but they look rather large on my tiny size 7 foot

Walking On Clouds. These shoes are extremely comfortable and make you feel like you are walking on clouds.  Takes a little to get used to but once you do they are very comfortable.  I know they are meant for walking/running but I tried them in the gym and unfortunately wouldn’t recommend.  Since the soles are so big to make you feel like you walks on clouds, they get in the way a little.  Tried them on the stair stepper and the leg press and they seemed to get in the way a little.  Would 10/10 recommend for walking and running short or long distances.  Good support, run true to size, haven’t had problems with them rubbing my heels,  and the fabric allows for good airflow so your feet don’t get too hot

Very disappointed. Ordered these shoes the day they dropped, and I was very excited for them. I tried them on as soon as they were delivered, and while trying them on the lace ring popped. Thinking it might be a defect I went to the store and exchanged them. Again when I went to lace up the replacement shoes the lace ring popped open. I reported the problem with the product to customer service, and I was met with being told that I would just have to return them from the store I got them from. Very disappointing. It’s unfortunate especially with the fact the shoes were comfortable when on, but if they can’t even handle being laced up then how are they going to handle multiple runs

Bold Comfort That Keeps Up With Me. I absolutely love my Nike Vomero Plus shoes! The bright pink color is bold, eye-catching, and always earns me compliments — they’re a statement piece in the best way. The design feels modern and energetic, with a sleek silhouette balanced by that plush, cushioned midsole.  Comfort is where these really shine. From running errands to clocking in 12-hour shifts on my feet, they provide amazing support and a soft, bouncy feel that keeps my legs and feet from aching at the end of the day. The ZoomX cushioning really does feel like running on clouds, and the breathable fabric keeps my feet cool even during intense workouts. The upper feels durable yet flexible, hugging my foot without any tight spots or rubbing.  If I could change anything, I’d make them just a touch lighter for even more agility on runs and perhaps offer a few more neutral or pastel colorways alongside the bold shades, for days I want something a little more understated. But honestly, they’re near perfect for my needs — stylish, comfortable, and versatile enough for both athletic performance and all-day wear

Vibrant, Comfortable, and Built for Performance. These shoes impressed me from the moment I put them on. The fit hugs the foot securely without feeling tight, giving just the right balance of support and comfort. The vibrant pink gradient design is eye-catching and energizing, perfect for adding a pop of style on and off the court. The fabric is lightweight, breathable, and kept my feet cool during intense sessions.  What stood out most was the springy cushioning that made every stride feel smooth and powerful. Direction changes felt stable and responsive, helping me move with confidence. If I could improve one thing, I’d add slightly more padding around the ankle for long training days.  As part of the Performance collection, these gave me both speed and comfort, making a real difference in my game

Plush Cushioning with Surprising Stability – Great for Long Miles. I picked up the Vomero Plus for daily road runs, and they’ve quickly become my go‑to. The fit runs true to size in my regular width, and even in the extra‑wide option, the lockdown feels secure without pinching. Despite the tall cushioning stack, they feel stable — I never got the “wobbly” sensation I’ve had with other max‑cushion shoes. The ZoomX midsole is the star here — ultra‑soft underfoot yet noticeably springy, which makes longer runs and recovery days a lot easier on my legs. The engineered mesh upper breathes well and wraps the foot comfortably, while the padding around the collar adds a premium feel. Style‑wise, the bold stack gives a chunkier profile, so if you prefer a super‑sleek look, this may not be your pick, but I like the mix of performance and standout design. At $180, it’s an investment, but the comfort, durability, and versatile ride make it a solid value if you log lots of miles

So comfortable!. These shoes are amazing. I’ve always been a fan of the zoom Pegasus, and they’re usually my go-to, but these Vomero Plus are on another level. They are so comfortable, even for my wide feet. I love the bright pink color and all the little details on them. I have a knee that likes to act up when I jog, but these shoes actually helped absorbe the impact on the ground, making it easy on the joints

Tons of cushion but still lightweight. I am utilizing these shoes for running. I run about 70% on the treadmill and 30% on pavement trails. I've been having some strain in my arches so I was excited to try different shoes. These Nike vomero plus shoes have significant cushioning while still feeling lightweight and free. I have not had any arch strain since using them. I love the wider toe box that makes my foot not feel constrained. The pattern is a bit bold for me but I don't mind it. I like the material as it is airy and light. I think these are great shoes and I'm excited to see how they wear over the next few months

Like running on pillows!. Once I stepped foot into these sneakers, I was immediately struck by how comfortable they are. They're super lightweight and they absorb your movements so you're well supported, which is obviously very important for running/exercising. I wouldn't call them narrow, but I have to wear thinner socks than usual so I would keep that in mind if between sizes. My feet run on the narrower side so I'm occasionally a 6.5, but usually a 7 in women's sneakers and these in size 7 were just right. I think the more I wear them, the more they'll mold to my feet so I'm not too worried about the slightly snug fit.  I wore them out of the box for a bit inside before taking them for a run outside, and thought they were super comfortable! I feel like I almost always have to deal with a spot on new sneakers that rubs against my ankle or something and takes awhile to get used to, but these were great right off the bat. I love the design of the mesh on the sides and top, very detailed and cute. The soles are SUPER bouncy which is kinda fun. I tried to move quickly in front of a mirror to see the soles absorbing my movements and it was really cool to see the soles bouncing back into shape after putting weight on them.  I'm always a little apprehensive about wearing new shoes for a full run outside, but these were really comfortable right away. I've been able to wear them for nightly runs on the street (mostly sidewalk, lots of hills) and they're in really good shape. Another great thing about these for running outdoors is that the hot pink color way is super visible. I run in a high car traffic area so having bright colored shoes is a major plus. Also, I love the design of the mesh—it's super cute, and it keeps my feet from getting too hot.  I'm usually the type to wear the same shoes for much longer than I should, usually because I get too picky, but I'm actually considering getting these in another color. The comfort is seriously next level!

Bouncy but super light! Pure comfort, pure joy!. Nike nailed it with these! I have wide feet but fit perfectly in these without a wide size. Super bouncy, lightweight, and breathable, plus I love the “Hot Lava” color. Not a tight sleeve design, so they adjust well with laces. Took a chance, glad I did!

Vomero Plus: Built for Long Runs and Long Days. The Invincible 3 is an awesome shoe — but the Vomero Plus takes things in a slightly different direction. It’s quicker off the ground, less bulky, and more streamlined overall. Perfect for long runs, yet just as good for spending hours on your feet. The Vomero 18 is great too, no doubt, but the Plus is just next-level comfort and performance. If you’re deciding between the two, grab the Plus — you won’t regret it

The Best Running Shoe. When I first opened the box, my initial thought was how cool these Nike shoes looked. The design really stood out, and they seemed like they’d be super comfortable. Once I put them on, I did feel a bit unsure about the thickness of the sole—I was worried it might make my ankles feel unstable while running. Thankfully, that concern turned out to be unfounded.  These shoes are extremely comfortable. The way the sole is built provides excellent grip on the ground, and I felt very stable, even on uneven terrain. I suspect the same when it’s slippery. What really surprised me was how much pressure they took off my knees, especially when running downhill. That’s usually when I feel the most strain, but with these shoes, I didn’t have any of that pain.  The fabric is soft and breathable, and the overall build feels durable. I can definitely say I’ll be reaching for these every time I go out for a run

Best Running Shoe I've ever bought. What a lovely surprise this was! Super comfy on long runs, easy runs yet still has some pop in them to pick up the pace. This can easily be an all in one daily trainer

Comfort Meets Energy. With over 25 years of running experience and countless shoe trials, I can confidently say the Nike Vomero Plus delivers exceptional quality. These shoes are true to size with a secure lace system that stays tight throughout the run. The toe box offers generous room for natural movement, and the breathable fabric. They are lightweight yet well cushioned. The shoes create a smooth, responsive run. The design offers excellent propulsion, making each stride feel effortless—almost like you are floating.  The Vomero Plus is a high-quality, comfortable, and performance-driven shoe that’s ideal for runners seeking a balance of cushioning, breathability, and energy return

Stacked cushion with Stability. As soon as I laced up the Nike Vomero Plus, I was struck by how secure it felt — even with its lofty cushioning stack. I’d been wary of instability common in high-stack trainers, but this model defied expectations. Walking post-run felt balanced and confident, not wobbly like others I've tried. After the run, I took my little ones to the park. Between chasing them around, bending down to pick them up, and bouncing around with them.. these kicks stayed comfy the whole time.  The color scheme was sharp, striking that sweet spot of bold appeal and everyday wearability. The materials felt noticeably higher quality than typical road running sneakers — the upper, padding, and overall finish felt great.  If you prefer a sleek look with your sneakers then these are not the ones for you the cushion definitely gives a wider/bulky look.   Whether you’re doing recovery jogs, daily miles, or just want that soft, springy ride without feeling like you're about to take a tumble—you’re golden

Amazing comfort. These shoes are my new favorite shoes. I am obsessed with them. I have an old knee injury that gets irritated when I run on pavement (and I happen to almost exclusively run on pavement) and these shoes have reduced my knee pain after running by at least 60%. They are so soft and springy but not so much that it makes me fall (there are some shoes that are so springy they affect my already not so perfect gracefulness haha). But these Nikes literally make my feet feel like they are being cradled by a cotton candy cloud of love. On days I don’t run I usually take a family walk for a couple miles through my neighborhood which has hills while pushing a stroller and these Nikes improve my stamina in my legs by cushioning the force of my steps when pushing uphill, I’m able to go farther without tapping out and my bike riding kids 100% approve. I didn’t expect to be so fully in love with shoes! I can’t wait to wear them on our trips to amusement parks, they help my knee pain so much, I’m super impressed. The fabric dries quickly as well, I thought I wouldn’t be able to wear them the following day after getting caught in the rain but they were fully dry by the next morning. They’re durable and still look almost brand new after daily wear and the quick drying helps keep and smells at bay iykyk. The only thing different I would recommend is get the shoe laces that don’t tie, I hate tying shoes that come undone when I’m out running so I switch the laces for the kind that are twisty that you don’t have to tie and then they’re PERFECT

The best max cushion shoe ever!!!. This shoe is a winner! It delivers on what you'd expect from a max cushion shoe with its soft yet responsive foam. The overall quality feels great, and it's exceptionally comfortable. The outsole is also a highlight, appearing to be very sturdy.  My only concern is with the outer material. I found the breathability to be lacking a bit

Nike Vomero Plus. The shoes are incredibly comfortable and functional. The fabric is soft and breathable and came in a super fun bright color. The inside felt spacious and roomy, not restrictive or tight at all. The support is awesome, helps almost propel you forward with every step. The treads on the bottom really help grip the ground better for running, on both the road and a treadmill. While the shape of the shoe is not normally one I gravitate to; I find it a bit “bubble” shaped- I love the performance of these shoes and would recommend them to any athlete who needs great support and grip in their sport

Like running on a cloud. These shoes are impressive. Like, I can't fully describe how impressive, impressive. I wear these shoes for errands and for walking/jogging on the treadmill, and no joke, these are the most comfortable running/training shoes I have ever worn... by far. The upper part of the shoe is extremely comfortable, pliable, and just fits around your foot perfectly. Then you take a few steps, and you feel the immense cushioning under your foot. Almost all shoes need a breaking in period of a couple days or longer to completely feel the comfort of brand-new shoes. Not these. The instant they go from box to foot, you know this shoe is not like the others. I don't feel foot and leg fatigue the same way when I am running on the treadmill. They allow me to do longer workout sessions, and I am not feeling the impact of the treadmill like before. Let me finish by saying that I cannot express this enough: these are the most comfortable shoes I have ever worn in my life

Super light weight shoes. These are great lightweight running shoes. I can really appreciate the engineering and design that was put into this shoe. You can literally feel how the rubber outer sole gives great traction while running on the asphalt or concrete sidewalks.  It’s really a game changer when it comes to running on the treadmill in the gym or running on the road. I enjoy jogs around my community and often times I’m doing trail runs. These shock absorption in midsole is amazing as I feel like I’m practically bouncing when I’m running! I can appreciate a lightweight comfortable shoe that also provides breathability in the outer mesh part. The color way I got is very bright and visible. I pair it up with an all black attire and the shoes really stand out in the best way possible. I always love a good pair of running shoes. These are highly recommended for the real runners at heart

Awesome and stylish running shoe. I love these running shoes! The Vomeros are super comfortable and very cushioned.The support in the shoe feels great and you feel supported while running. The material is high quality and a great, fun color! feel the support when running. It is a really good fit for me, true to size. It’s only been a few runs so far, but I do feel they’ve helped my performance

Puts a Spring in Your Step!. These shoes are very comfortable and make it feel like you’re walking or running on air! The bright pink color is very fun, especially for a running shoe. Running in the Vomero Plus’ is smooth and it feels like they help propel me forward which is great, especially on runs where I feel like I’m having an off day

So great for long runs!!!!. I’m currently training for a marathon and have been looking for a comfortable shoe to wear during my long runs. I am a big fan of cushion and having a nice, soft shoe. The Vomero Plus is PERFECT! When I first put it in, I instantly felt how it hugged and secured my foot. It felt like I was walking on a cloud! When I started my run, instantly noticed the shock absorption and how every foot strike felt protected. I even felt like I was getting a bit of energy return back, too! I didn’t feel like I was too high off of the ground either. After the run, my feet felt the usual soreness, but they were definitely less sore than how my feet usually are in other shoes I run in. Can we also talk about the style of the Vomero Plus?! This is a shoe that definitely stands out. In my opinion, the brighter the better, and this shoe delivered! My entire group gave me compliments on it and asked me where they could buy it!  Overall, this shoe is amazing and is definitely now in my shoe rotation for my marathon training

Not what I expected…. These things are CHUNKY! Personally not a fan of all of the cushioning, I can see how they are meant to be used to enhance performance but for a non Olympic athlete just using for daily running and gym, these are just too much for me. They take some time to get used to with the heel to toe rock. Size wise they fit as expected. Picture for reference on the sole of the foot compared to af1s

Finally a worthy max cushion runner from Nike!. I can’t begin to explain how satisfied I am with the new Nike Vomero Plus! A little backstory, I am a big guy that works on my feet all day long and after work I still like to find time to get out and walk and jog. I need a maximum cushioned shoe that can withstand a lot of weight and have good enough quality that will hold up over time, all the while offering that recovery comfort after a long days work. Nike has delivered with the Vomero Plus. The fit is true to size, and the quality on the upper has that premium feel compared to the Vomero 18. The real star of the show is the full length ZoomX foam midsole. When I say pillows for feet it is the only thing I that could compare. ZoomX offers the soft plush feel you need on your long runs but also offers that bouncy responsiveness you know and love with traditional Zoom.  I am making the Vomero Plus a part of my daily rotation, that is how much I truly enjoy them

Running on a Cloud. The Nike Vomero Plus shoes exceed standards of both comfort and style! I use them for my daily jogs, and have been surprised by how comfortable they are (making it easy to go the extra mile). The added support provides more elevation than I expected (I actually look taller), but it is also what sets them apart in terms of comfort. The colors are vibrant, and the attention to detail is impressive - even the shoelaces match the shoe’s color profile. These will be my go-to for both everyday wear and running

Total win!. The Nike Vomero Plus is hands down one of the most comfortable running shoes I have ever worn. The hot pink color is bold and fun, and the sleek design gets compliments every time I wear them. From the first step they felt like walking on clouds. They are lightweight, cushioned, and perfectly supportive. My first run was pure comfort with no blisters and no break-in needed. These are a total win!

Foot Heaven!. Words cannot describe the fit and feel of this shoe! After running in the Nike Vomero Plus I knew this would be the only shoe for me! I suffer from plantar fasciitis and arthritic knees. I walk or run daily on the boardwalk by the shore. Lately,  there have been days I couldn't finish my workout. I thought I needed to replace my regular workout shoes. What I actually needed was a new brand of shoe! As soon as I put on the Nike Vomero Plus I knew this was the answer to my foot problems. The shoe is so comfortable but it also offers arch support. I was dancing in my living room because this shoe felt so good!. I decided to try it out on my workout. I was able to run 5 miles and knew I could go further!  The shoe itself is truly a work of art! After my run, people on the boardwalk were complimenting me on my shoes! They are a beautiful bright pink and orange. I do run or walk at night and I feel safer with the bright color. What I like most about the Nike Vomero Plus is the comfort and support. My feet do not ache after my workouts anymore. The fabric is light and my feet can breathe. I honestly would not change anything about this shoe! As an older person with arthritis these shoes are the answer I've been looking for!

Plush Cushioning Meets Everyday Versatility. Having run in the Nike Vomero Plus, I can genuinely say it’s one of the most comfortable shoes I’ve ever laced up. From the first step, the full-length ZoomX midsole delivers a soft, bouncy ride that feels luxurious without being sluggish. It’s perfect for recovery runs, long miles, or even casual wear when you want that cloud-like comfort.    The upper is breathable and padded just right—no hot spots, no pressure points. I especially appreciated the roomy toe box and secure heel fit. Despite the high stack height, it doesn’t feel unstable, and the grip from the waffle outsole is solid even on damp pavement.    It’s a bit of a splurge, but the comfort and durability make it worth it

Comfort Galore. Have you ever ran on marshmallows? Neither have I, but I imagine this is how it would feel. Comfortable, springy, lots of cushion, airy and light. It even took me a second to adjust after putting my feet in because i’ve never been used to stepping in a shoe so nice.   It’s definitely true to size and fits well; I often opt for wide foot shoes because they’re more comfortable to me, but It wasn’t necessary with this shoe. Support is top notch.   The design is really nice, the color is AMAZING. I couldn’t stop staring at them when they came out of the box, even my husband was jealous.   I went on a run with them, a dirty run at that, and the dirt wiped off really well later when I went to go clean them, so I appreciate the fabric and material used.   I think overall the comfort, purpose and eye- catching color really made me love this shoe. I don’t really have complaints. I recommend highly!

Superb Cushioning With Decent Stability. Given that each runner’s stride is unique, I need to offer some context about my own before reviewing these shoes. I have struggled with weak Achilles tendons for many of my running years. Additionally, I have high arches, especially in one of my feet. Hence, I have always looked for shoes that offer the rare mix of great stability and high cushioning. I had stayed away of the Vomero because, even if the cushioning seemed great, they were not as stable as I would have wanted them to be.  Now, with the Vomero Plus, I have finally given this line a chance, and I should say that they did not disappoint. They truly deliver the cushioning they advertise, and then some. It really does feel like running on a very soft surface.  With that that said, as a runner that looks for stability, how did these shoes feel? Well, they were not great, but definitely not bad either. For all the cushioning they provide, the stability is decent, and the sole is also very responsive. Even with my weak ankles, I did not feel these shoes wobbly or slow. Do not get me wrong, I would not categorize them as stability shoes, but at least they do not seem to sacrifice too much stability to offer all that cushioning.  In conclusion, if stability is not a concern for you, and you are looking for a cushioned shoe, then look no further. I loved how soft the Vomero Plus felt. In addition, they are responsive enough to make you feel faster than when running on other cushioned shoes. If you want cushioning and stability, I would still favor another model, but I would argue you can still add these to your shoe rotation. With my Achilles tendon issues, I would not run my longer runs on these, but I would definitely use them for my midweek recovery runs

Stepping on clouds. Very appealing orange almost salmon color. Very light, use them for everyday use and feel almost like UB from you know. One thing I will mention is the narrow sole, if you have jello ankles I might look for alternatives. The design/color combo are definetly a looker and have gotten compliments

Super Comfortable rebounding kicks!. The shoes are great! The cushioning and comfort is next level, just the right amount of padding on the ankle and tongue, and the breathability is nice.   The design of the shoe is great the hints of the Nike swoosh under the toe box see through layers is really cool.   The stack of the Cushing on the side goes higher than the foot bed so does give some lateral stability.   Reflective hints throughout from the front Nike logo on the toe box, small lines throughout, and a reflective rear pull tab.   The orange color way is nice especially for early or late night runs.   I have wide feet and the shoes fit perfectly.   I’ve been using them as casual shoes and they are great but even when running the responsiveness is great. You can feel the zoom cushioning push you forward

New Favorite Medium Distance Runner. I’m indecisive when it comes to max cushion shoes for various reasons, yet these address most if not all of them. The uppers of the shoe are breathable yet supportive, a balance that not many other shoes have. With skinny feet, I have issues with lockdown in some shoes, but the thick tongue and heel counter take care of this. Without tying the laces too tight, your foot feels secure and comfortable. Air can easily flow through the upper to cool your foot on a hot day.   One of the biggest issues I have with maximum cushion shoes is stability. Typically I’ve found that when a shoe has as much cushion as these, it feels unstable and like it’d be easy to roll an ankle. That isn’t the case with these at all. While you still feel the extremely cushy outsole and higher off the ground, you feel firmly planted. When running, walking, and standing, I never had an issue.   The color is a polarizing, vibrant orange. While it makes it harder to match, it feels suited to running, as you’d hardly ever wear these casually. The silhouette is super sleek and pleasing to the eye, the subtle curves and layering really make it a good looking shoe. The fabric choices feel breathable yet durable, with super thin fabric to the middle and a thicker material to protect the end of the toebox.   TLDR: I think it’s a very well put together shoe, and I’m not sure I’d change anything about it except for the color. It checks all the boxes, and is my favorite max cushion shoe I’ve ever worn. Just in the few weeks I’ve been running in them, I’ve noticed a considerable reduction in the impact of striking the ground and my joints have felt better. I would absolutely recommend them for anyone doing casual runs

Like Walking on Clouds. I gave these to my daughter that took up walking this year as a hobby. She’s been wearing the wrong shoes for this activity as she starting walking longer distances and even started jogging a bit.   Enter the Nike Vomero Plus- she loves these! They are like bouncing around on clouds. She started walking in them and has now moved us to running on a regular basis due to the spring action she gets in these. They have been super breathable in this hot summer heat and even though they have a higher stack, they are amazingly light. Plus we love a vibrant colorway. The only issue she has noticed is a little tightness in the toe box when climbing higher elevations but I think that can be solved with going up an half size. All in all, I think this will be a great running shoe for Nike. Now I have to get a pair for everyone else in the house!

Great running shoe!. Great choice for running shoes! The shoe is lightweight, comfortable, supportive, and has a good amount of cushion. Love the color and design of the shoe. Especially the different tones of orange. I also like the sheer mesh look. It fits true to size and I still have some wiggle room. This is now a go-to shoe for my easy and long runs!

Speechless.. The Vomero Plus has been the softest running shoe I have ever felt! At first, I was skeptical of the height, but once I put them on, I understood the amount of cushion that was being provided. Even though they were higher in height, I never felt unstable and landing on the cushion felt incredible! My first run with the shoe not only felt great, but I also felt myself speeding up in pace with the response coming from the shoe, which is very noticeable! Loving the design and colors as well! I would highly recommend grabbing these if you enjoy a cushioned, responsive, and stable ride!

Vomero Plus: HV8150-801. The Vomero plus are an ultra-plush cushioned masterpiece. The ZoomX midsole is loaded with cushioning, making them my comfiest shoes in my rotation. I have been using them as dog walking and running errands shoes, my feet having been loving the switch from retro Jordans to these. You will instantly notice how responsive the cushioning feels when you take those first steps and the wider footbed your feet will live in. I highly recommend these for anyone who wants a pair of running shoes and wants something that feels like running on a spongey cloud. These will be true to size, but recommend ½ size up if you are planning on running, walking, or just being on your feet for long periods of time!

Just when I thought the cushioning couldn't get better!. I have used the Invincible Run 3s for a long time, but the first time I took these on a run I instantly fell in love. As someone who does frequent recovery runs to maintain fitness after my Sunday soccer games, these definitely get the job done! Running shoes that have max cushioning like these Vomero Plus are easy on my knees and ankles so I feel like I am not over-exerting or causing any injury to myself. This orange color is also fire-- I think I found my new perfect match!

The REAL Deal. Wow I am obsessed! These are pure comfort the second you put them on. I wore them all day shopping last weekend and my feet were still happy and ready for more by the end of the day. I felt secure and cushioned all day long. I was getting questions and complements all day too! The bright orange really makes you stand out. I love the little accent swoosh on the toe from the aerial view, nice touch! I went with my usual size for running shoes and they fit perfectly. I will definitely be getting another pair!

Like clouds!. These new Vomeros are awesome! I’ve ran in them a couple times so far, and they are like running on clouds. I have been using old Nike trail runners for a while, and the difference I feel with the Vomeros is incredible. To be fair, I had been using the trail runners on concrete all summer…but I digress.  The fit is great. They are true to size, and of course, full of cushion. The fabric is breathable and lightweight. I wouldn’t change anything about the shoe so far. I’ll update this after I’ve put some more mileage on them

The MOST Comfortable Sneaker. I’ve worn a lot of different Nike silhouettes over the years, and I mean it when I say the Nike Vomero Plus is easily the MOST COMFORTABLE sneaker I have ever put on my feet—and it’s not particularly close. I wore them for the first time when I knew I was going to be on my feet for a good portion of the day, and they felt amazing! They are unbelievably lightweight, and the chunky foam midsole is so soft under foot, giving me incredible cushioning.  I’m not a serious runner by any stretch, but I went for a short jog throughout my neighborhood, and these delivered in every capacity! I’ve never really worn proper “running shoes” before, and now I have something that I can take anytime I’m going for a light run or hitting the treadmill. The mesh upper also has a lot of flexibility without feeling flimsy; I do wonder how the sneakers will hold up with extensive wear, especially for avid runners, but I didn’t see any issues after a full day on foot, so I have no doubt they’ll be perfect for my use.  As for the design, I love how bright these are in the Orange Pulse and Hot Lava colorway. The black accents help bring out the vibrancy of the sneaker, and as somebody who has worn plenty of interesting models and colorful kicks, I enjoyed how they looked on me. I can understand how they might not be for everybody, so hopefully they make more neutral color options. Plus, I just want another pair in a more minimalist style so I can have a diverse rotation of Vomero Plus styles to choose from. The comfort is just THAT good.  If you’re wondering about the fit, I went true-to-size, and they were perfect. No heel slippage, no tightness or pinching anywhere, just pure comfort

BRAVO(mero), NIKE!. Nike has introduced a new tiering system with their three main Daily Trainer lines; the Vomero, Pegasus and Structure. The Vomero is known for its neutral CUSHIONED ride. The Vomero Plus is the second tier in Nike’s Vomero line-up. It continues its legacy and ventures off into new territory, as it’s first one with FULL ZOOMX cushioning.   FIT: TRUE TO SIZE. I opted for the usual size I opt for Nike, and the Vomero Plus does not deviate, so stick with your regular size.   DESIGN: The Vomero Plus appears to pay homage to the ICONIC Air Max 97 with the wave-like design on the upper. As the first Vomero with FULL ZOOMX cushioning, the ZOOMX on the midsole is the highlighted CHUNK. There are also small reflective details for visibility in dark areas. The reflective logo on the outsole lip is actually a nice detail. The laces are secured and stable in a Flywire-like system but are positioned internally just above the tongue area. You can barely see the ends of the Flywire. This system does a superb job at containing my feet throughout my runs.   FABRIC: The Vomero Plus’ upper is constructed of different materials. The predominant material is something reminiscent of Vaporweave but softer. I found it a pleasure to run in. There were no hotspots. The heel padding is plush just like the ride you would expect from a Vomero trainer.   RIDE: After about running ~60 miles in the Vomero Plus, I am A HUGE FAN! I have not had a Nike solidify a spot in my running rotation in a LONG TIME. Even though I have WIDE CATALOG of trainers to choose from, I find myself reaching for the Vomero Plus almost every time!   The ZOOMX in the Vomero Plus is not the same as you would feel in the Nike’s elite racing footwear, but it still exceeded my expectations. This trainer is meant for those recovery, slow and daily miles; however, not only does it excel at those type of runs, it also has the ability to push the pace comfortably even without a propulsive component in a carbon fiber plate or a Zoom Air unit(s). The versatility of the Vomero Plus makes it an easy choice over other trainers that I have. I find myself really enjoying my run for the sensation after every stride.     LIKED THE MOST: The Vomero Plus really shines is all categories that I look for in a daily trainer. It looks amazing, the materials feel plush, and, most importantly, it performs amazingly well for me! To narrow it down to what I liked the MOST is how the Vomero Plus performed during my runs! It does what it’s supposed to do with easy runs, recovery runs and daily at an A1 level. AND it unexpectedly handles faster paces really well!   CHANGES: One minor critique that I have is that the high contact areas on the outsole wore down quicker than I expected. Perhaps using a different compound for the outsole would prevent the accelerated breakdown. I would also prefer Nike not to use the nubs on the outsole.   CLOSING: Overall, Nike has a CLEAR winning product in the Vomero Plus. It really checks off (get it?) all the boxes when I consider purchasing a running trainer; it looks good, it’s constructed of plush materials and its ride makes running an absolute JOY! Not only that, it’s the first Nike in a LONG TIME to clinch a position in my running rotation as my primary daily trainer! BRAVO(mero) Nike! You are so back!

Comfortable running shoe for long or short runs. The Nike Vomero Plus is incredibly comfortable and provides all the support needed for a good run at the gym or at home. This shoe is well-made and sturdy and has held up like new after 8 days of cardio, showing minimal wear and tear and feeling just as comfortable as the day I put them on . The extra padding throughout the sneaker ensures a comfortable feel as you run for longevity or speed.  The plush fabric feels very luxurious and I love the bright orange color. One of my favorite features is the zoomx sole that leaves every step feeling as comfortable as when you started even when you are finished with your workout.  The one complaint I have is that the sidewalls do feel a little tight, but I blame that on wearing the shoes tighter and finding a good comfortable fit for when I was running for endurance purposes. Overall, this is a comfortable shoe that allows me to run for long distances and still feel comfortable after the run is over and done with. The shoe even prompted me to try more cardio because the comfort level and I frequently experience either leg or back pain after continuous cardio days and this shoe has made it so none of that has even occurred. A great running shoe that I will be using for years to come

I’ve had some of Nike’s more hardcore running shoes and there are in a nice sweet spot between comfort and having the support to go on a long run.   I thought the supple feel of the uppers felt really nice on my feet and the huge foam on the midsole provided a cloud like walking experience. It also feels like there is a little spring in the step similar to the carbon plate in an Alphafly but not as hardcore. The toe box being slightly elevated also encouraged a smooth gait which makes walking with these for an extended period of time very pleasant.   I didn’t love the color of my particular pair, the orange is very bright but there are other colors available that I would happily pick. I also have questions about the durability of the outsole, I don’t know how long the foam will hold up but only time will tell.   Overall I highly recommend these kicks as both an everyday wear and a training shoe

After having gone without a pair of everyday shoes long enough, absolutely no doubt about it, it was time for me to get another pair of shoes. Upon purchasing my very first pair of Nike Air Force 1, a classic, all-white (low), I had finally realized all of the reasons that this sneaker is still one of the most popular shoes available today. This review will evaluate the AF1 of its Design, Comfort, Durability, Versatility and Overall Value so that I can provide an honest opinion on how these shoes are for everyday wear. The Air Force 1 was the first ever basketball shoe to feature Nike Air technology, revolutionizing the game and sneaker culture forever. Over three decades since its first release, the Air Force 1 remains true to its roots while earning its status as a fashion staple for seasons to come. I ordered the shoes straight from the Nike app and they had come in surprisingly quick. The design of the box wasn't very special, but it was classic and stood out. The first look of the Air Force 1 has a pure white look that grabs your attention. The shoe is smooth leather and has subtle Nike swoosh. The all white look gives that sense of cleanliness of the shoes. The shoe cuts just below the ankle gives a good shape that works well with many of my outfits. What I like most is how the shoe is stylish without being super showy. It’s a shoe I have been able to wear in school, going out, or even more dressed-up events.  My first experience with putting on my pair of AF1s was that they were way more comfortable than I expected. I had heard a lot of reviews about this shoe from people before I bought them, I believe they are a pretty comfortable shoe. There is definitely a lot of cushioning under your foot that is soft while you walk in the shoe. Although the shoe is a little heavier than your typical sneaker. If you haven't ever put your foot through a new pair of shoes for several hours, you will discover that just after sitting for a while, they will start to feel just a little bit weird, but after about a full day of wearing them, they will start to fit naturally on your feet and will not cause any issues when worn for long periods of time. The comfort of the shoe is essential to everyday sneaker wear. If you plan to be wearing AF1s regularly, you will certainly enjoy the comfort. One of the reasons I think the cost is justified is because of the durability of these shoes. The leather resists quick wear-and-tear and the creases do develop at the top of the shoes. The creases can be helped by buying a crease protection which I had to do. After wearing my shoes for about a month I have still been able to keep them clean. The laces are pretty easy to keep clean but dust and dirt are a great attraction to them.  Overall, the Nike Air Force 1 has definitely earned a place in my regular rotation of shoes. I am definitely glad I decided to finally get AF1's. Its classic design goes great with a lot of my outfits. They also have been a shoe I can use for nice events or even just dinners.

Usually with an Air Force 1, I have to size down 1/2 to 1 size. The Flyease version sizing was a bit odd for me, as the shoe width still felt the same, but there was extra room in the toe box lengthwise. I had to fully lace them up to eliminate heel slippage, and before I did that, they felt very clunky and bottom-heavy, more than the standard AF1s already feel. After a while, the clunkiness got a bit tiring, so I ended up switching to a more comfortable (broken-in) shoe.  I can't see myself wearing these a lot (in part because they're super white), but I do like that I can just slip them on and off without having to worry about the laces. If you truly have mobility/dexterity issues and want to wear an accessible shoe that doesn't look any different from the OG, this should work for you
