Good for high arches and to prevent heal strike pain. The shoes are simple design with a fun pop of green color.  They have a chunky looking sole, so they gave me a little extra height which I like at only 5'4'.  My feet were a little sore the first few times I wore these shoes, but it was because they actually fit my high arches well.  I am not used to it and realize how much better my feet feel overall.   In addition, I walk heavily on my heals, but the slightly rounded bottoms along with the inside shape kept me from being able to strike my heal down while I walk and jog which was really great.  The shoe are great for my good weather walking / jogging as well as running errands

Perfection. Oh my goodness!!! These are my absolute favorite new pair of shoes. The minute I saw them I knew they’d be comfortable and versatile. I wore them to work and I felt like I was walking on memory foam all day! It’s literally like an airy feeling. They provided me with great support and comfort as I go up and down stairs and walk/run during my shift.. they are not one bit tight. So you could probably wear a semi thicker sock. But I just used regular thin socks. Another plus, if you’re short like me they provide you with about an extra inch of height hehe.  I loved the green bottom of the shoes as well. The only thing I would change is add a little strap so you can pull the shoe on easier

Perfect Running Shoes!. Extremely happy to say I found my new favorite running shoes! They have the perfect amount of padding and support for me to feel comfortable running miles with them. Pretty stylish (black & white) so you can technically wear them with anything. If you like the Nike pegasus or the 270s, then you will love these!

All day long. I could wear these shoes all day long, and I have! They are so comfortable. I love the shape of the bottom too, it has a slight curve unlike any other shoes and it oddly keeps you wanting to move more and more! I wouldn’t change a thing about these shoes, they are by far my favorite shoes out there right now!

Walking on Clouds!. Having worn just about every brand of running show (brooks, asics, hoka) I can confidently say that these shoes are the most comfortable. The thick sole makes it feel like you are walking on clouds. The heel drop is perfect for running as well.   The shoe fits true to size and has plenty of room for lateral movement. The style is spot on as I have received many compliments while wearing them.   My biggest complaint is I do not have a lot of information on what this shoe was designed for. Is it a cross trainer or running shoe? It seems to do everything well but I would like to know its specific purpose.   Overall, I will definitely be looking to get another pair of the Motiva's when these wear out!

Quality You come to Expect from Nike. After a few weeks with the Nike Motiva shoe I have found it to be a solid product. I run around with my kids outside and at night after work and they've shown to be both durable and comfortable once broken in.   Despite running a bit small, or maybe thinner than other shoes, once broken in a bit, they remain comfortable all day long. It took me a week or so personally to feel more comfortable and that they weren't restrictive and have since worn them a bunch. My running isn't distances but more being active with my family. They did recently just get quite a workout at O'Hare airport as I ran back and forth trying to catch my flight. (I caught it).  Overall once I got past the first week or so of breaking them in, they have become great every day shoes for when I know I am going to be a bit more active or busy. They also add an inch to my height and as a man under 6 foot, I can appreciate that too, haha.  They are a bit bulky if you need to pack them anywhere, but otherwise was a great all around shoe

Perfect jogging shoe!. Love the comfort, and the bounce of the shoe! It took a little time to get used to, but it was great when walking or jogging because it cushioned my steps so well, even when my feet were hitting the pavement hard. The foam part of the heel is starting to crease a little bit so I don’t know how long they will last, but they are worth repurchasing because I am never in pain after wearing them. This is an awesome sneaker!

Supportive shoe, but not for my wide, no arched feet. I received the Nike Motiva a few weeks ago and have been trying them out in different forms of light jogging and walking. I love their sleek look and design. They are a well made, sturdy shoe. The material is soft and, in my few weeks of trying them out, seem durable. There is great support and cushion in the shoe. The shoe is designed well to propel you forward in light exercise.  Unfortunately, the Nike Motiva were not a great fit for me. I typically wear an 11.5 shoe. I received this size, but my feet still felt tight in the shoe. I do have wide feet with no arch. The Nike Motiva is a narrow shoe with great arch support. My toes still had plenty of room, so I don’t think it was a size issue. The Nike Motiva simply is not a shoe for wide feet and no arch. They would, however, be great for someone with narrow feet and high arches.  I wanted to love the Nike Motiva; however, it’s design is specifically for narrower, high arched feet (the exact opposite of my feet)

Feels amazing. Looks okay.. This shoe is insanely comfortable. The perfect combination of flex and fit. The sole is perfect and the arch is fantastic. The only thing I didn’t love is the shoe looks like something my Grandpa wore in the early 90s. Not something I’d grab for every day wear

Our gardening shoes. Receiving these shoes was like pulling a spaceship out of the box in our crunchy, neutral-toned home. But boy did our 4yo daughter love them! She was tickled to have “exercise shoes” just like mom and dad and couldn’t wait to try them. While they are a bit bold for our aesthetic, she seemed to enjoy the few hours she spent in them and didn’t complain about any discomfort. While I wasn’t exactly doing laps in them myself, the rigid frame in back seems like it could make certain play difficult or a day at the zoo tiresome on the heels… but alas that’s speculation. So far they’re serving time as gardening shoes as the bottom tread doesn’t accumulate too much mud (and therefore doesn’t get tracked inside)… a win for Moms and Little Girls alike! Not sure that we’d pick these off the shelf again… but for the time being they’re a great addition to DO’s closet. Thanks Nike!

Extremely comfortable after initial adjustment period. I’ve never worn running shoes with such thick padding before and this was a revelation. There is an adjustment period to being so much higher off the ground for lack of better words, but you mostly feel it standing still. In terms of material and comfort, it’s not as bad as it looks weight wise, and way more comfortable than it appears. I hope the cushioning lasts a while as I’d love to keep these in the rotation for a long time

True COMFORT. These shoes are SO comfortable! I walk around a lot for work and I can wear these all day without feeling any discomfort. They are supportive and fit just right! The sizing is definitely true to size, I went with my usual size in Nike shoes and had no issues.   These shoes are shaped for real comfort, they are well cushioned and resemble walking on a cloud! They have a very unique shape that literally encourages balance and proper posture. I love the classic black and white design which easily makes these shoes my daily drivers!

Great runners. These shoes are amazing for running and are very comfortable. I wasn't too sure about the curve on the bottom of the shoe, however once I started to walk around in them, I didn't have some of what I call normal pain from repetative movements, I get from some more of my other shoes. I think the combination of the curve and also how the cushion inside hugs your feet have a lot to do with this. It took me a second as well to adjust to running in them however once I did, that became an easy task as well

Super comfortable. I was hesitant about these when they came in but they're extremely comfortable. They go well with an every day fit with jeans and with my gym fits also. I'm happy with the sizing, they fit as expected

The best shoes my husband has worn!. My husband LOVES these shoes! He is a self proclaimed gym rat who spends a lot of time doing cardio at the gym and has tried many running shoes. As soon as he put these on he said they were the most comfortable shoes he’s worn and didn’t need the inserts he usually puts in his shoes. Then the next day after he went to the gym I got a text that they are the best shoes!   They fit true to size and are very stylish. My husband says he would wear them all day! Highly recommend!

Great shoe for walking. These shoes were very comfortable to walk and stand in. Walking was especially comfortable with the rocking sole. They didn’t require any breaking in. These would be great for a runner as well.   The shoe was also very well designed and great looking. Overall I would recommend this shoe

Pleasantly pleased with a different type of shoe for me.. I am normally a one shoe kind of guy. I have been buying the same tennis shoe for about 10+ years. With this new Motiva shoe, I was surprised how comfortable I was.  My intention was to just use them as workout shoes but they have become my daily walking and wear shoe. I now use them daily for my walks while pushing the double stroller and when my kids request a slow stroll or fast mode, my feet feel comfortable and able to go from walking to running in these Nikes. I have always been a white shoe wearer so the white and black fit my style perfectly. Being able to feel the cushion and support under my feet as I move is my favorite part. Only gripe is as a wide foot guy they can be a little tight on the little toe side

All around excellent. I am reviewing the Nike Motiva on behalf of my husband, who received a pair. At first when he pulled them out of the box, he wasn’t keen on the style because the heel is really thick, but once they’re on, it isn’t obvious at all. He’s been wearing them to the gym the past week or so and has loved them! He mostly lifts and does some light cardio on the elliptical and has relayed to me that these are great for both. This shoe runs true to size and the 9.5 (his usual size), has been fitting perfectly. The design is all around great. Aesthetically speaking, I love the wavy sole because it gives it character in a sea of gym shoes. The Motiva also has the right amount of support and he loves the shock absorption. When I asked him what he’d change about them, or what he didn’t like, he said that they’re all around great and he wouldn’t change a thing. I think men may be easier to please than women in the footwear department, ha! I can’t speak too much on durability yet because he’s only been wearing them for a couple weeks, exclusively to the gym, but I’m happy to do a follow up down the road! He is notoriously hard on his footwear (and everything else), so I’m hoping they can hold up. He says they’re super comfortable, so I’m sure, at some point, he will start wearing them as a primary shoe instead of just gym wear. That will be the true test of comfort and durability. As of right now, they are perfect in every way, according to him

Comfortable for running and cute for errands. Love the look and feel of these shoes. I usually wear between a woman's 9.5 and 10. I got a 10 and it has a little room which is perfect because I like a little room when I run. When I first put them on I was surprised by how elevated of a platform it seemed to have. I was worried it wouldn't be great for running, but I loved it. I only run between 1 ans 3 miles at a time and they really flowed with each step and proved to be very comfortable. I have very flat feet and these seemed great to me. I love the updated yet classic look and find myself grabbing them whenever I run errands too because they are so comfortable and cute

Takes a little getting used to, but absolutely worth it!. I’ll be honest, I have waited as long as possible to write this review, as I feel like my opinions and experiences have changed (for the better) since I first started wearing these shoes and have come to appreciate them as I wear them more and more.   The first thing I noticed about the Motiva’s was their look, and they look good! They have a very streamlined almost stealth design with the lacing eyelets and design elements flowing seamlessly into the body of the shoe. Combined with the lowkey branding, I feel like my shoes can make a statement without feeling like a walking billboard. The materials are fantastic, with the upper being super breathable, and the thick sole providing lots of cushion. Those materials lead into how comfortable they are around my foot. Everything just fits, and they feel fantastic.   Where I feel I got off on the wrong foot, was with the sole. The sole is slightly curved, being thicker in the middle under the arch of my foot and thinner at the heel and toes and creates almost a rocking sensation when standing. Additionally, the sole has a ripple wave pattern, that provides a slightly different sensation than a more traditional pattern. As a result, I felt slightly unsettled in the shoes at first; almost a little off balance as I was getting used to how they feel.   I’m over that now.   As I kept wearing the shoes, I realized that the curved design was helping keep pressure off the balls of my feet and heel. I started to feel more grounded in them, and the slightly curved design really kept me from “plopping” my super flat feet against the ground. I now almost feel propelled forward on every stable step, and I felt more cushion in my step than other shoes. As I continued to wear them, I have found myself enjoying them more and more, with the comfort of the upper now being matched with the sole.   As a result, these have become my go-to daily shoes, and have no issues wearing them all day. The weather hasn’t been cooperating as much as I would have wanted to get outside and run or hike, or even hit the pickleball court, but I look forward to as we continue to move into the spring and I continue to feel more and more confident in these shoes.   I’m not sure if I would change anything in the design of the shoe, other than potentially making the curve of the sole slightly less pronounced to speed up getting comfortable in them.   In summary, the Nike Motiva’s are fantastically comfortable shoes. If you’re not used to a slightly curved sole, you may have an adjustment period, but it will be worth it!

Walking on clouds. These sneakers are definitely unique. At first glance, thry look like your average pair of Nikes. The difference lies in the soles. They have what it feels like is a tremendous amount of cushioning. I usually opt for airmax, but these are actually even more comfortable. There is a bit of a learning curve in walking on them in my opinion though. The cushioning seems to be emphasized greatly in one area. I did have to train my balance yo be accustomed to these, however they are very comfy to walk in

Great running shoe. These shoes fit true to size and feel good for running. The soles are funky and overall are nice looking shoes. They are comfortable to wear while moving. I like that they propel me with each stride to help with my run. My only negative is that they aren’t the most comfortable when not in motion, while quite comfortable when in motion

Like walking on a plush mattress!. As these are men’s shoes, I interviewed my husband on his experience wearing them. As a gym shoe lover, he felt these were extremely comfortable for a walk or short run. The cushion under foot was like “walking on a plush, comfortable mattress!” They felt very secure, like they wouldn’t slip off if he stumbled while running. They hugged his feet but didn’t feel hot and fit true to size. They should be easy to clean as Nike’s usually are. He rated these a 4 out of 5 stars as he felt the shoe overall was a bit too heavy for wearing for an extended period of time. The style/design looked good but was a tad bulky for his taste

Bold soles for a very comfortable running shoe. These running shoes are incredibly comfortable! I can see how the curved design of the soles is intended to continue to aid in forward progress while running/walking, but it takes some getting used to. Otherwise, the design of the shoe is right on par with something I’d select any day - solid black and white, classic, and breathable material. They are certainly comfortable enough to wear all day, but as I mentioned, the curved soles are a bit awkward for anything other than running (or walking a long distance). They are also slightly heavy and clunky, hence the 4-star review. Overall I’m impressed and excited about the ingenuity and what it means for future running shoes!

Running Dynamics. I really like these so far. When first standing in them they feel a bit like you’re “rocking” from back to front but once you’re running they’re great. They provide the stability and support in the right places to allow for a proper stride and will hopefully help to save my knees in the long run! They’re comfy for every day wear as well and do fit true to size in my opinion. I also like the neutral colors and design, they actually look nice as compared to some others

Shoes made in Heaven. These shoes truly feel like you're walking on the clouds. I can't say enough about how comfortable they are. I have been wearing these daily to work long hours, where I'm always on my feet and my feet have never felt better. The platform base of the shoe is unlike any I've ever worn before and I was afraid they would be uncomfortable. That was the furthest from the case. These shoes are the most comfortable pair I've ever owned. Highly recommend if you're looking for an insanely comfortable shoe

Super Unique Sole. This shoe is SO cool. The base of the shoe is quite tall and the cushioning is soft but not too squishy. It really feels like it has formed to my foot after a couple of weeks. The sole has a very unique curve so that if you are standing straight, you can rock back and forth like a rocking horse. It is a very fun feeling and honestly they make great walking shoes for that reason. Very breathable fabric yet it still feels thick and firm. They are my casual shoe of choice as well as my go-to walking shoe because they are just so comfortable!

Motiva is Awesome!. Alright, so right out of the box the shoe is super unique, from its sole to the material used for the sides and tongue. I have to admit I was a little skeptical at first thinking that balancing would be a issue when walking or running.  After my first few outings with them however I was able to start breaking them in and found that are super comfortable and even helped with some pain from tweaking my knee during lifting. Great product, I would highly recommend

Nike Motiva. I love the look of these shoes but was so disappointed that they run a bit small! I would recommend sizing up a half size. Other than that, they are the perfect show- super supportive, interesting looking and very comfortable
Great comfortable shoe. This shoe is so comfortable. Great for walking and jogging with a nice casual look

Great Shoe for Walking and Running!. The fit of this shoe is true to size, but because of the elevated sole- definitely feel more cushioned with each step. The curved sole would be good for walking/running, but not great for wearing all day long. And the mesh-like top has great stretch and breathability. It’d be cool if the sole of the shoe wasn’t as emphasized and blended in to the foot part or maybe a solid color for the whole shoe

Running with style. The Nike Motivas are a uniquely designed running shoe that combines a 90s style design, with a contemporary twist. The subtle zebra style lines on the body of the shoe, alongside a well placed lace ribbon give these shoes more design than your average running shoe. Although the shoes run slightly on the smaller side, I have no doubt after breaking them in, I can clock a number of miles in them. My only knock on these is the white foam base wrinkles/seams quickly, and the soles rounded edge won't be for all runners. Overall though, these are a solid pair of shoes with the quality and precision we have grown to expect from Nike

Great shoes for walking and jogging!. At first I was skeptical about these shoes. When I first put them on they felt weird on my feet because they have a rounded sole. It was a weird feeling to not have my whole foot on the ground at the same time. However, once I began walking and jogging in them I realized that this design is perfect!  The rounded soles allow your foot to easily go from heel to toe when walking or jogging. The fit was perfect, I'm a size 11 and the size 11 fit just right. I also am really enjoying the comfort of the shoe. I not only wear them when walking and jogging but have also been wearing them around the house and work. I would highly recommend these shoes to anyone looking for a shoe to walk or jog in. They would also make for a great pair of sneakers to wear for someone who is on their feet a significant amount of time each day

Style + Comfort to MOTIVAte. Day 1- Got my new pair of Nike Motiva shoes (size 10).  First impression - love the design of the shoes and black logo on dark upper making it subtle, yet the contrast in color between dark upper top and white high foam stack makes it right amount of bright.  Getting the foot in is more snuggly then I liked it to be but once I wore the shoes and tied the non-slip laces, it is very comfortable to wear.  I personally like the shape of the front of the shoe which gave my toe all the room they need. Breathable upper kept my feet cool.  Day 2- Like it more than day 1, arch of the foot is supported extremely well and gives comfort for wearing all day long.  Day 3 and counting- This pair of shoes fit like a hand in a glove and has quickly become my favorite go to pair for outdoor active workouts- walking, jogging, etc.  It also motivated me to ditch the car few times for running the errands nearby. What else can you ask from a shoe? :)

The Nike Motiva are a game changer!. The Nike Motiva shoes are an absolute dream! Every element from top to bottom has been carefully designed with both style and comfort in mind. The fit is absolutely spot-on and wearing them feels like stepping into a cloud. It's hard to put into words how amazing the cushion feels - whether you're walking or jogging, your feet will thank you. Even the slight arch in the sole takes a moment to get used to, but it's a small price to pay for such incredible support. I seriously can't find any fault in these shoes - they're a true masterpiece. If you're searching for the perfect pair of shoes, look no further - the Nike Motiva is where it's at!

Supportive Running Shoes. - The shoe fits well and feels slightly wider that most Nike running shoes.  The sole is soft and provides good cushion when walking and running.  The curve of the sole helps with the running motion, but can make it slightly awkward when standing still until you get used to the shape.   - The shoe has a very progressive design that sets it apart from most running shoes. The material is lightweight and comfortable when running.  - The shoe is comfortable to wear, but the curve of the sole takes some getting used to.  When moving, the shoe helps with your stride and is very comfortable.  - I like the support that it provides your heel when running the most as it feels very sturdy and promotes a good stride. The design of the shoe with many angles is slightly more elaborate than I usually pick, but the shoe looks very modern

Nike Motiva adds a little bounce to your step. These shoes looked great straight out of the box - the design is great and the "wavy" bottom is really unique. The first time I wore the, it took a little adjustment - the bottoms aren't flat and it felt like I had a little extra spring in my step - kind of tough to explain, but almost like they intentionally force you to center your balance differently than "normal" shoes.  I feel like I have average width feet and these fit me perfectly - snug, but not too tight. I've worn them for long periods just hanging out, walking quite a bit, and during a little bit more intense activities and they were comfortable in all situations.  Overall - I'd recommend the Nike Motiva and fully expect these will be my go-to shoes for a lot of different activities

Something to get used to.... I am writing this review on behalf of my husband, whom these shoes were for. My husband is about 6'3" and 215lbs. He is a creature of comfort and fairly particular on what he wears. Since he is tall, the thickness and waviness of the sole made him feel as though his center of gravity was a bit challenged. He described it as almost a "rocking" sensation. While the shoe felt comfortable and durable, he wasn't expecting the "rocking" feeling of the sole of the shoe. As mentioned, he is a very predictable and particular person when it comes to what he likes, this is to no fault of the shoe itself. He did say the shoe fit great, and did not feel too small or too big for his size 13 feet. He feels as though this is a shoe he could probably wear all day, comfort wise, but it would definitely take some getting used to. The style of the shoe is black and white, a crisp and clean look that is versatile enough to wear on a run, or with a pair of jeans. It's a great shoe if this is the look and feel you are going for, but my husband is more of the predictable type and we took one star off because it is something he would have to get used to wearing, which he sees as a bit of an inconvenience. We prefer a shoe to "feel right" and as an extension of the foot, not something to train our treads to get used to. Another reason we knocked off a star is because the shoe looks somewhat like an orca. It was the first thing he thought of when looking at them and slipping them on. "Killer whale" vibes on a guy that dresses for comfort over fashion. The larger sole and design of the shoe is a bit trendy for us, but would honestly be perfect for a person looking for that kind of thing since the shoe is otherwise comfortable, durable, and versatile

Very solid running shoe. The Nike Motiva is the most unique running shoe I've ever had, specifically because of the shape of the sole. The curvature of the sole seems to create an inclination to move forward with each stride. All of the running shoes I've used before were pretty flat (I have a fairly normal, even stride when I run).   The most noticeable thing for me as I've been getting used to this shoe is that it does seem to result in me using muscles in my legs which I often don't. Specifically, I feel more pressure in and around my shins and less on my calves. However, none of this was uncomfortable or has led to any pain or shin splints, it's just a new experience for me.   The shoes are very comfortable, if maybe a touch on the heavier side than I'd like in a running shoe. They feel snug on my feet, they look stylish (I had a solid black shoe with white soles), and I'd definitely add them to my running shoe rotation

Motiva tennis shoes. Good shoe for moving. I chose this shoes on whim for a new shoe for walking. I am new father and frequently take our daughter on long walks.   I really enjoy these shoes for a few reasons. They are lightweight and have a good Cushion to them which makes for a good walking shoe and general moving around shoe.  The main unique feature on this shoe which really was the reason I like this shoe is the sole. It's unique convex sole which almost makes you move forward while wearing them. I have not wore them too much running so can't say for sure if it'd feel the same as walking, but for walking it is a great shoe to get you moving!  One thing to note, since the convex design is unique it is not something your foot/muscles will be used to at first. It took me a few times wearing then to get used to the motion but now a couple dozen times going on long walks I don't notice it.  Additionally, because of the design I would classify this shoe best for moving and not for lounging or simply walking around the house.   All and all I appreciate the design, functionality and genral aesthetic of the shoe and what it has done for my walks. I think I would continue to wear my running shoes for my runs but this will be my walking shoe for the future

Nike motivas. So when I gave these a try it was a first for me but I heard the design was good for improving movement and stride. The rounded soles took some getting used to but within a week or so they were comfy and really took the impact out of running and walking and helped with a more fluent stride. Over all I enjoy the shoes and would recommend them to anyone who enjoys going on walks or runs to help them and make it a more comfortable experience

Great shoes, but not very sturdy.. These shoes are very comfortable and very attractive. The fit was very nice, and after a full day on my feet and some time at the gym they were as comfortable as you can get. However, upon taking them off I discovered a large mount of creasing the the sole. Considering I had only worn them for a whole 10 hours, I feel like the won’t hold up well and will wear down quite quickly
Classy dress up or down. Got the Chocolate and Pink luv'em
Low Profile Love. I love this shoe!! I was looking for a less bulky yet “Shokz” type shoe and I stumbled upon these!! Considering another color too!
New shoes. I’m on my feet at work 8 hours i love these shoes very much they make my feet look smaller im going to buy another pair in a different color for the summer

Womens Nike Shox Z. The Nike Womens Nike Shox Z was definitely a pair that needs more work or further advancement in the technology and material being used. Additionally the feel of the shoe felt very stiff and outdated, therefore the comfortability also missed the mark. The material and color of the shoe also felt outdated and needed more life to it. Probably a shoe I wouldn’t recommend to anybody

Girls Shox Rocks. I love the shoe! The material feels premium and well made, its redesign keeps the classic Nike Shox look but in a version that’s perfect for the ladies! I like that the shoe is narrow and slim, it gives it an easy to style look with anything from sweatpants to denim to shorts and long skirts. This shoe is quickly becoming one of my go to favorites and I love the monochromatic look of the silver pair. The feel molds to your foot after a few wears, once you walk around in these you won’t want to take them off! Looking forward to seeing more colorways and versions of this one!

Teacher Approved. I work on my feet all day long and these shoes have helped support my bad knee. They fit true to size and give a cushion to each and every step. They do add a lift which not only took away some of my pain but it also made me feel taller. Along with being so comfortable, the model also has a sleek silhouette that elevates a variety of looks.    The traction is useful especially when coaching and trying to keep up with middle school volleyball players inside a slick gym. I've worn these shoes consistently over 12 hour days and have yet to feel discomfort or foot soreness. They breathe fairly well which keeps my feet from stinking and the true to size fit makes sure that my feet always feel secure. My only minor complaint was I felt like the laces didnt stay tied as easily as some of my other shoes but that may have just been a personal issue.   So if you are looking for a shoe that can support you through the wear and tear of daily life, then these are definitely the shoes for you

Retro Shox. Take a walk down memory lane with these Nike Shox Zs. The design is sleek and eye catching while giving nostalgic vibes. Super easy to style, dress them up or down, depending on your fit of the day.  The sizing runs true, but full disclosure, they’re definitely on the narrow side. For the soccer players out there, they fit a lot like cleats without the studs, snug and form-fitting. They’re pretty stiff right out of the box, but break in gradually and become more comfortable with each wear.  While they work great for casual wear, they also feel well suited for gym or lifting sessions. Overall, this is a solid pick if you’re looking to combine sporty and stylish with a classic throwback look, just keep the narrow fit in mind if you’ve got a wider foot

These shoes are something great!. These shocks truly fit to the foot in such a natural way. Walking in them feels like walking on air. I think the fabric is comfortable on the foot while still being supportive. The top of the shoe seems a little narrow for my foot so that is the only thing I would change

Classic. The Nike Shox is such a classic shoe, you really can’t go wrong.  From the slender look, the comfort, and the shox effect, it has been a staple in my closet for over a decade.  I wear a 7.5 in womens; this shoe fits a bit looser than my other shox but not enough to size down.  I prefer extra room so that I can wear any socks without compromising the fit/comfort.  These are my go to when I know I need to be on my feet all day.  Love these shoes!

The shox are making a come back. I love that the Shox line is coming back! I love the refined and modern look of these shoes and that they appear less bulky than older Shox models. The shoes are more on the narrow side, but I have narrow feet, so it works for me. I love the color and the shiny accents throughout the shoe and the springy heel cushioning. I like that I can wear them both casually and sometimes in a little more formal settings. I was able to wear these all day after breaking them in, but wouldn't mind even a little more cushion throughout the shoe. One thing to note, the back of the shoe comes up a little high, so I have to wear higher socks so it doesn't rub. I do feel like the material will help these shoes last a long time, and overall I am a big fan

Silver Surfer. The Nike Shox Z is a standout sneaker that delivers both in form and function. Right out of the box, the comfort level is impressive. The fit is generous, offering ample room in the toe box and along the sides, making it easy to slip them on without a struggle. Whether you're heading out for a casual walk or picking up the pace with a light jog, these sneakers provide just the right amount of support and cushioning for all-day wear. What truly sets the Nike Shox Z apart is its striking design. The sleek silhouette, paired with a bold silver finish, makes a strong visual statement. One of the most eye-catching features is how the shoes catch the light — they almost seem to shine, adding a subtle yet stylish flair. The fabric is both soft to the touch and built to last, striking a fine balance between comfort and durability. Perhaps the best part about the Nike Shox Z is its versatility. While it has a casual, everyday feel, it's designed in a way that works well in multiple settings. Whether you're dressing down or adding a sporty edge to a more polished look, these sneakers can adapt effortlessly. Overall, the Nike Shox Z is a well-rounded shoe that combines everyday comfort with head-turning design. It’s a solid choice for anyone looking to add a bit of boldness and flexibility to their sneaker lineup

So sleek!. The best words to describe these are sleek! Can't go wrong with the legendary Shox! Great look that can go with all of my wife's workout gear. She was looking for some new training shoes. She loves the details throughout the shoes. Her only complaint is that they feel a little tight (width). So, if your feet are on the wider side, I would go up a size. Overall, these are some great training shoes!

Ultimate Streetwear. The Nike Shox Z is true to size. The sleek design is on the narrow to medium sign. Reminding me of a cleat without spikes.  The mesh textile fabric is a little harder not making the show ideal for a fun. It is also less breathable but comfortable enough for all day wear. Actually wore them to work in a pair of pants and polo style shirt and got several Compliments. The metallic and reflective design is surely to make your outfit pop and stand out above the rest. There is also a strip in the middle of the show that maybe suede so I would keep that in mind for where you decide to sport your sneakers

Super happy to see the Shox Back!!. I love that Nike is bringing back the Shox style. It brings back so many good memories. I love the design of these specific Shox, as they are my favorite Shox style I've seen. They are super cool looking. These Shox fit true to size. They are a tiny bit narrow, but I have narrow feet, so it's not a problem for me. They are a little stiff and not the most comfortable shoes I own, so while I wear them around just fine, I'm not able to wear them if I'm going to be on my feet all day. The fabric is very high quality, though, and very sturdy, which makes me believe they'll last a long time. I do wish that they had a little bit shorter and skinnier laces, as I think that would make them look even better. However, I love the silver color and it is very different than anything I currently own

Very sleek. The Nike Shox Z are a classic pair of sneakers. They are comfortable for all day wear and you can style them easily with a pair of jeans too. I love the silver accents. They have a sleek and narrow shape.   I’m a solid size 8.5 and these ran big for me, I would go down half a size for sure

Shoxingly Amazing!. Nike somehow keeps improving old tech without sacrificing too much. The Shox Z is a minimalist approach to regular Nike Shox and has a very high-fashion look and feel to them. They are loud without being too obnoxious. They say, "look at me" without drawing too much attention. The fit is the slightest bit narrow but you won't go wrong if you go TTS. If you have wider feet, you may want to go 1/2 size up. These are super comfortable to run errands and lounge around in all day. I haven't worn them for rigorous activities such as running or exercising yet but they hold up really well for casual wear. You can wear them up with designer clothes or wear them down with a jogger outfit. I know a lot of Europeans are in love with this silhouette and now I understand why. If you are looking for an every day pair of shoes, these shox might be what you are looking for. Highly recommend you give these a try!

Overall, the VS Pace 2.0 Shoes are a great option.. The VS Pace 2.0 Shoes are a solid choice for those seeking a stylish yet comfortable sneaker for everyday wear. Here’s a breakdown of what you can expect from these shoes:  Design and Aesthetics: The VS Pace 2.0 shoes have a sleek, minimalist design that works well with a variety of outfits. The synthetic leather upper gives them a polished look, while the subtle branding and clean lines add to their modern appeal. Available in several colors, they can easily complement casual, sporty, or even slightly dressier attire.  Comfort: These shoes are designed with comfort in mind. The cushioned insole provides decent support, making them suitable for extended wear. However, some users might find that the shoes could use a bit more arch support, especially if worn for long periods or for activities involving a lot of walking or standing.  Durability: In terms of durability, the VS Pace 2.0 shoes hold up reasonably well for their price range. The synthetic leather upper is resistant to scuffs and easy to clean, while the rubber outsole offers good traction and durability. They may not be the best choice for heavy-duty activities, but for everyday wear, they perform admirably.  Fit: The fit is generally true to size, though those with wider feet might find them slightly snug. The lace-up design allows for some adjustability, ensuring a secure fit.  Price: One of the strongest selling points of the VS Pace 2.0 shoes is their affordability. They offer great value for money, combining style and comfort at a price that won’t break the bank

The contrasting colors create a sleek and eye-catching look that effortlessly complements any outfit. The clean lines and modern design elements give these shoes a contemporary edge, making them a versatile choice for both casual and athletic wear.  Comfort: When it comes to comfort, the Adidas Pace 2.0 shoes do not disappoint. The cushioning provided by the OrthoLite sockliner ensures a plush and supportive feel with every step. Whether I'm walking or engaging in light physical activities, these shoes offer excellent comfort and minimize fatigue. The breathable upper material also helps to keep my feet cool and dry throughout the day.  Performance: The performance of the Pace 2.0 shoes is impressive. The rubber outsole provides excellent traction on various surfaces, giving me confidence in my movements. The lightweight construction allows for agile and responsive footwork, making them suitable for activities such as running or gym workouts. These shoes offer a good balance of flexibility and stability, enhancing overall performance.  Durability: In terms of durability, the Pace 2.0 shoes are built to last. The high-quality materials used in their construction, combined with solid stitching, ensure long-lasting wear. Even after regular use, these shoes show minimal signs of wear and tear, proving their durability and value for money.  Conclusion: In conclusion, the black and white Adidas Pace 2.0 shoes are a fantastic choice for those seeking a stylish and versatile pair of sneakers. With their timeless design, exceptional comfort, reliable performance, and durability, these shoes offer great value for the price. Whether you're looking for a casual everyday shoe or a reliable athletic companion, the Pace 2.0 shoes are a reliable and fashionable option that won't disappoint

Unhappy. After an impressively long vigil waiting for a particular product to be discontinued, my moment finally arrived. I made the purchase immediately.  The package embarked on a mysterious journey of its own and, after about 20 days, was officially declared "lost" by Adidas. I was informed I would receive a refund and a goodwill voucher.  There is, however, a small arithmetic dilemma: the refund and voucher combined no longer cover the cost of the shoes, as they have since been reintroduced to the market at full price.  When I brought this ironic twist to Adidas's attention, I was assured they would "learn from my case."  While I am of course thrilled that my experience will serve as a valuable educational module for a global corporation, I must admit I was hoping for a slightly more tangible graduation gift. Thank you, Adidas, for this unique lesson in advanced patience and pricing paradoxes

Very poor quality. I bought a pair of Adidas SL 72’s in the London store a few weeks ago. Initially loved the look, however they made a strange, squeaky noise which I thought was due to the fact that they were new. The noise wasn’t particularly an issue as I liked the look and feel of them. Fast forward less than a month and the sole on one of them is coming away. Furthermore, one of the stripes on the side is also coming away, so really disappointing quality. What was worse was the attitude of Adidas staff when I tried to get the matter resolved. I was referred to WhatsApp and the response to my complaint was to contact the store. I did this, but the lady on the phone insisted that I return them to the London store. I live a good 2 hours from London and it would cost me the same in the value of the shoes getting there. After I tried explaining this, the woman didn’t listen and clearly couldn’t care less. I am therefore left over 90 pounds out of pocket with a pair of trainers which are less than a month old and falling apart. I am a 51 year old man and all I have done in them is walk; certainly no hard yards to make them fall apart. Disgusting quality and frankly disgusting customer service from a company who clearly couldn’t care less. I shall take my business elsewhere in future!

Would return if I could. I have a few things to share about these shoes unfortunately not very favorable for adidas. Maybe it's just my bad luck and I got a defective pair somehow but anyway this is not something one would expect when buying directly from adidas. I would return these shoes with no hesitation but I have worn them a few times and I have discarded all packaging so I'm kind of stuck with it.   Believe it or not, but the left and right shoes have visible differences. Not visible enough for somebody to notice immediately but once you see them, it's very hard to unsee that and it's very annoying. Check the attached images. - The right shoe is slightly wider than the left - The stitch on the back of the left shoe has a visible bump - The right shoe suede quality is inferior, it's rougher than the left one. I wonder if adidas quality control would agree with me on my observations?  Additionally, the size is slightly larger. I've been always wearing 10.5 from adidas and never had an issue. These shoes are not 10.5 but more likely 10.75. They are not 11 either so if you think you could go down half a size there's no guarantee they will fit. However, this size inconsistency is noticeable and discomforting.  On the bright side, the shoes are beautiful. Cool retro shoe with a dope colour way. The SL 72 RS is a great looking shoe that combines a vintage aesthetic with some modern upgrades.  Here's a quick breakdown:  Pros:  Sleek design with a mix of suede and nylon for a classic vibe. Comfortable and lightweight thanks to the EVA midsole. Good traction from the rubber outsole. True to size (for most) Cons:  Can be a bit narrow in the toe box. If you have wider feet, consider going up a half size. The tongue can be a little stiff, especially with low socks. Overall:  A stylish and comfortable shoe that's perfect for everyday wear.  Just be aware of the potential sizing issue and stiff tongue if you have sensitive feet

Great trainer with great discount. I purchased this product as it appealed to me by the colours - mainly my favourite - and was on offer with a nice big % off, so much so that I allowed myself the indulgence of personalisation which was an added feature. The customisation process was easy and accurate and being able to choose emojis too (although a small range) was fun. The ordering and payment process was smooth, email communications too. The product was delivered on time to the store I selected, and packaging was sturdy and undamaged. I would add that I questioned if the outer box that the trainers box was inside was really necessary though, thinking about material waste. The trainers inside were fresh, quality check found no faults and custom personalities printing on the sides was well done and smart. Once tried on the foot fit was a bit tight and a squeeze, however at the end of a day out and about wearing them the comfort prevailed and I was satisfied they would be comfortable going forward. Overall I am very happy with this product. Thanks Adidas!

Too good to be true!. The adidas NY 90 shoes bring a nostalgic, retro-inspired design with a modern twist, making them a standout in the brand’s lineup. Inspired by the street culture and fashion scene of the ‘90s, these sneakers feature a combination of premium leather and suede for a stylish yet durable construction. The upper is complemented by adidas’ signature three-stripes, adding a touch of authenticity and heritage. The cushioned EVA midsole ensures comfort and support, making them suitable for daily wear or casual outings. With a sleek silhouette, the NY 90 shoes are versatile enough to pair with jeans, shorts, or joggers, easily blending into any casual or streetwear look. The rubber outsole provides reliable grip and stability, while the overall design remains timeless, evoking the spirit of ‘90s New York fashion. Whether you’re an adidas fan or simply looking for a comfortable, stylish sneaker, the NY 90 offers a perfect balance of vintage appeal and contemporary performance

Worst Exchange Service Ever! Month Later, No Shoes. Adidas’ customer service and exchange process has been extremely disappointing. I initiated an exchange nearly a month ago and have now been waiting over three weeks for a replacement pair of shoes, with no clear timeline or sense of urgency from the company.  I don’t understand why a simple mail-in exchange should take this long, especially for a brand of this size and reputation. The lack of efficiency and transparency in the process is frustrating, and the delays have left me without the product I already paid for.  This has been one of the worst exchange experiences I’ve encountered. Adidas seriously needs to reevaluate how they handle mail-in exchanges, because this process is far from customer-friendly. I expect better communication, faster turnaround times, and a system that doesn’t leave customers waiting indefinitely

Initial impression after a short run: - MIDFOOT: foam is dense exactly like Adios Pro 3’s as expected, good enough grip with the Conti, no slipping and sliding on damp pavement with autumn leaves, but could’ve been grippier - FOREFOOT: feels very close to the ground, which I don’t love - HEEL: a little unstable especially at slower paces - UPPER: really don’t like the tongue, laces are thin and kinda annoying but do stay in place, material doesn’t feel premium, and could use a heel tab as putting them on is a bit fiddly - WIDTH: as someone who has normal width feet, this feels a bit narrow in the middle, with my feet bulging out a little to the side - RIDE: feels a lot better at faster efforts vs easy paces, but kinda miss having some stiffness - COMP: it’s not a comp for the Superblast 2 as initially thought, which still feels more premium and better all around (incl stability and grip) especially for long runs, probably closer to Rebel v4, Novablast 4 or Pegasus Plus - OVERALL: i do still really like it, would be interesting to see how it breaks in over time and looking forward to taking it out on some longer miles
